item.description: 原文:
item.author: ben

item.title: MongoDB CRUD 数据库操作🍃 | The MongoDB CRUD Database Operations🍃
item.description:
本文向您解释了 MongoDB CRUD 操作，任何数据库中的四个主要数据库操作。 它们使您能够从数据库中创建、读取、更新和删除数据。 创造

这就是您将新项目添加到数据库的方式。 这些项目可以是对象或单个项目。 如果该集合不存在，此操作将为您创建一个新集合。 在这种情况下，集合是指您的数据库的名称。 下面是一个如何将项目插入名为students 的数据库的示例。 您可以同时插入一个或多个。

读

此操作使我们能够从名称本身读取数据库中可用的信息。 我们使用一个名为 find 的方法来执行此操作。

在括号内，您输入您正在寻找的特定项目。

更新

此操作有助于对已经存在的数据库进行更改。 在括号内，您将指定要交换的值对或要在其中添加的值对。

删除

删除集合中不需要的对象或项目。 与插入命令类似，您可以同时删除一个或多个。

我希望这能让你的生活更轻松。

快乐编码！🍃​☕​



原文:

This article explains MongoDB CRUD operations to you, the four primary database operations in any database. They enable you to Create, Read, Update and Delete data from your Database. The CRUD acronym stands for;

C- Create

R -Read

U -Update

D -Delete

Create

The C stands for Create. This is how you add new items to your Database. The items may be objects or individual items. If the collection doesn’t exist, this operation will create a new one for you. Collection, in this case, refers to the name of your Database. Below is an example of how you insert items into a database called students. You can insert one or many at the same time.

db.collection.insertOne()

db.collection.insertMany()

Read

This operation enables us to read the information available in our Database from the name itself. We use a method called to find to do this.

db.collection.find()

find with no query inside returns the entire database.

db.collection.find(query)

Inside the bracket, you feed the specific item you are looking for.

Update

This operation helps one make changes to an already existing database. Inside the brackets, you will specify the value pairs you are interchanging or the ones you’re adding inside.

db.collection.updateOne()

db.collection.updateMany()

Delete

Delete the object or items that you do not want in your collection. MongoDB provides the following methods for deleting. Similar to the insert command, you can delete one or delete many simultaneously.

db.collection.deleteOne()

db.collection.deleteMany()

I hope this makes your life easier.

Happy Coding!🍃​☕​
item.author: kithminiii

item.title: 聊天机器人第 1 部分：NLP 的幕后 | The Chatbot Part 1 : Behind the curtains of NLP
item.description:
我开始这个博客系列的目的是记录我构建功能性聊天机器人应用程序时的所有步骤。 然而，在撰写本文时，我已经将一个在 Flask 后端提供服务的聊天机器人模型连接到了一个 React 前端。 结果只用了几天。

我在 React 方面的经验和对客户端-服务器模型的熟悉使我能够相当轻松地连接模型和前端。 然而，构建聊天机器人的逻辑我从来没有费心去理解。 这是跟随教程的最大陷阱——你倾向于尽快看到成品。 现在，让 GitHub Copilot 自动完成大部分代码并没有帮助。

从那以后的日子里，我有时间反思整个过程。 尽管本文最初旨在涵盖构建聊天机器人模型所涉及的所有步骤，但这样做甚至会破坏完成所有这些的目的。 将理论与代码与实现分开是非常重要的。 所以我们从理论开始。 我向你保证，这绝对不会无聊！

自然语言处理 - 简称 NLP - 是人工智能聊天机器人的核心。 一个正常运行的聊天机器人应用程序依赖于模型理解人类语言及其复杂性的能力。

现在，计算机以你看到的数字进行思考和交谈。 那么如何在人类语言和机器语言之间进行翻译呢？ 这是我们深入了解 NLP 背后的一些核心概念的地方（基本上是我在尝试分解聊天机器人的功能时发现的所有新东西）

词干化与词形还原

词是复杂的东西。 它们的含义可以根据上下文而改变。 从一个句子中取出一个词，什么都不会改变。 或者一切都可以。 作为人类，我们已经习惯于自然地区分所有可能的变化而无需思考。

但是你怎么能教机器所有这些东西呢？ 简短的回答是——你没有。 相反，你教它根据模式行事。 你帮助它了解如何与你交谈。 根据您输入的性质回复您。 毕竟，这是构建聊天机器人的唯一目的，不是吗？

现在，如果是这样的话，我们可以自由地抽象出所有使语言如此复杂的细节。 如果我们从这个词中得到的只是它添加到句子中的上下文，我们不能减少说“gener”的慷慨和慷慨吗？ 如果我们还处理与一般等词可能混淆的问题，我们确实可以。 这是“词干”的核心——你将一个词简化为它的词根——也称为它的“引理”。

词形还原基本上做同样的事情。 但是，有一个关键的区别。 我不想自己解释，而是向您指出有关堆栈溢出的答案：

最后一点非常重要。 根据您的用例，您可以随意选择其中一个。 当我们到达那里时，我将讨论实施。

词袋

假设我们已经使用我们的样本数据完成了词形还原（或词干化）。 我们的模型现在应该知道要寻找什么了。 一个词汇。 请注意，它所知道的只是您对其进行的训练！

但是聊天机器人需要理解完整的句子。 这些句子可能包含多个不属于其当前词汇的单词。

我们将如何处理这个问题？ 再次记住，目标是让模型现在以某种方式为句子分配上下文——不多也不少。 我们需要根据其有限的词汇量找到一种方法来做到这一点。 这就是“词袋”（BoW）概念的用武之地。

尽管我更愿意将实现细节留到以后的帖子中，但如果不这样做，就很难深入研究 BoW 的概念。

聊天机器人应用程序——本质上是大多数 NLP 算法——执行所谓的分类。 这正是它听起来的样子。 您将输入分组到几个可能的类之一（在我们的例子中是“上下文”） - 预先准备好的类。 例如，我们可以构建一个仅理解上下文“问候”和“再见”的聊天机器人。 请注意，这些上下文纯粹是任意的。 知道了这个细节，我们就可以继续讨论一个词袋是如何在事物方案中变得直观的。

所以你有两件事 - 你的词汇和你的课程，即上下文。 当我们在 NLP 的情况下处理监督学习时（我们明确地训练它如何理解它的世界），我们也有我们训练它的数据（一组句子，每个句子都标有上下文）。 例如，假设我们的词汇表包含引理“hi”、“bye”、“fly”和“bear”。 假设我们的课程是“问候”和“告别”。

给定我们有标签的数据，我们获取我们拥有的每个句子，计算我们知道出现在这个句子中的单词的出现次数，即我们对参数（我们的单词）进行加权，并将该权重分配给训练数据中定义的标签。 每个句子基本上都归结为一个“词袋”。

例如，在我们的例子中，假设我们有句子“嗨！你今天早上好吗？” 在我们的训练数据中，带有“问候”的标签。 我们注意到它包含我们词汇表中的一个词——“hi”。 因此，参数“hi”的频率为 1 的句子必须将“greeting”类设置为 1，即“greeting”为“true”，“farewell”类为“false”/0。 从本质上讲，我们在尝试保留上下文感的同时将句子简化为数字。

使困惑？ 你可能想再读一遍。 或者看看这个超级有用的视频：

语料库

诚然，了解语料库的含义对于理解 NLP 或聊天机器人的工作原理并不重要。 然而，它是在类似项目中经常出现的术语之一（也是你可以随意抛出的那些花哨的词之一，以暗示你真正了解你的东西的人——不管你是否真的了解你的东西！）。

本质上，语料库是您标记的文本（或音频）数据——您最终用于训练模型的数据。 但是，它也可以扩展到用于对单词和句子进行词形还原的文本正文。 基本上任何带有标签的文本（或音频）正文。

结论

因此，我已经剖析了所有对于理解基于 NLP 的聊天机器人如何在引擎盖下工作至关重要的理论。 在接下来的文章中，我们可以（最终）深入了解实现和编码细节。 与往常一样，请随时让我知道您对这篇文章的看法。 并且看到大部分内容是根据我自己对事物的理解编写的，如果我不在任何地方，请纠正我！



原文:

I started this blog series with the intention of documenting all my steps as I built a functional chatbot application. However, at the time of writing this article, I've already connected a working chatbot model served on a Flask backend to a React frontend. It ended up only taking a couple of days.

My experience with React and familiarity with the Client-Server model allowed me to connect the model and frontend rather easily. However, the logic of building the chatbot I never bothered to comprehend. This is the biggest pitfall of following tutorials - you tend to get caught up in seeing a finished product as soon as possible. Now, having GitHub Copilot autocomplete most of the code did not help matters.

In the days since, I've had time to reflect on the whole process. Although initially this article was meant to cover all the steps involved in building the chatbot model, doing so would have defeated the purpose of even going through all this. It is very important to separate the theory from the code from the implementation. So we're starting off with the theory. I promise you it'd be anything but boring!

The basics of NLP

Natural Language Processing - NLP in short - is at the core of an AI-powered chatbot. A functioning chatbot application relies on the model's ability to comprehend the human language and its intricacies.

Now, computers think and talk in numbers you see. So how do you translate between human and machine language? This is where we get into some of the core concepts behind NLP (basically all the new stuff I've come to discover while trying to break down how the chatbot functions)

Stemming vs Lemmatization

Words are complicated things. Their meaning can change based on context. Taking a word away from a sentence and nothing could change. Or everything could. As humans, we've become attuned to naturally distinguish between all the possible variations without a sliver of thought.

But how can you teach a machine all these things? The short answer is - you don't. Instead, you teach it to act based on patterns. You help it understand how to talk to you. To respond to you based on the nature of your input. After all, that is the singular purpose upon which a chatbot is built is it not?

Now if that is the case, we're free to abstract away all those fine details that make language as intricate as it is. Cannot we reduce generous and generosity to say "gener" if all we're deriving from the word is the context it adds to a sentence? Provided we also deal with the possible confusion with words like general, we indeed could. This is at the heart of "Stemming" - you reduce a word to its root - also called its "lemma".

Lemmatization does basically the same thing. However, there is a key difference. Rather than explain it myself, I'd like to point you to this answer on stack overflow :

That last point is very important. Based on your use case, you're free to go with one or the other. I'll go over implementation when we get there.

Bag of Words

So say we've done our lemmatization (or stemming), using our sample data. Our model would now have an idea of what to look for. A vocabulary. Do note that all it knows is all you've trained it on!

But a chatbot will need to understand complete sentences. These sentences will likely contain multiple words that are not part of its current vocabulary.

How would we deal with this? Again, remember that the goal is to get the model to now assign context to sentences in some way or the other - nothing more and nothing less. We would need to find a way to do this based on the limited vocabulary it has. This is where the concept of "Bag of Words" (BoW) kicks in.

As much as I'd prefer to leave the implementation details to a future post, it'd be rather hard to dive into the concept of BoW without doing so.

A chatbot application - and essentially the majority of NLP algorithms - perform what is known as classification. And that means exactly what it sounds like. You group an input into one of several possible classes (in our cases "contexts") - classes that are prepared in advance. For example, we can build a chatbot that understands solely the contexts "greeting" and "goodbye". Note that these contexts are purely arbitrary. Knowing this detail, we can move onto how a Bag of Words becomes intuitive in the scheme of things.

So you have two things - your vocabulary and your classes i.e contexts. As we're dealing with supervised learning in the case of NLP (we train it explicitly how to understand its world), we also have the data we train it with (a set of sentences each labelled with their context). As an example, say our vocabulary contains the lemma "hi", "bye","fly" and "bear". Say our classes are "greeting" and "farewell".

Given we have labelled data, we take each sentence we have, count the occurrences of the words that we know that appear in this sentence i.e we weight the parameters (our words), and assign that weight the label as defined in the training data. Each sentence essentially boils down to a "bag of words".

For example, in our case, say we have the sentence "Hi! How are you this morning?" in our training data, with a label of "greeting". We notice that it contains a word from our vocabulary - "hi". So the sentence with the parameter "hi" having a frequency of 1 must have the "greeting" class set to 1 i.e "true" for "greeting" and "false"/0 for the class "farewell". Essentially, we're reducing sentences to numbers while trying to retain a sense of context.

Confused? You might probably want to read all that again. Or take a look at this super helpful video:

Corpus

Admittedly, knowing what corpus means isn't critical to understanding how NLP or a chatbot works. However, it's one of those terms that tends to come up a lot in a similar project (and one of those fancy words that you could casually throw around to hint to someone that you REALLY know your stuff - regardless of whether you actually do or not!) .

Essentially what a corpus is is your labelled text (or audio) data - the data you ultimately use to train your model. However, it can also extend to the bodies of text used to lemmatize your words and sentences as well. Basically anything that is a labelled body of text (or audio).

Conclusion

So I've dissected all the theory that would be essential to understanding how an NLP-based chatbot works under the hood as best I understand it. In the following post, we can (finally) dive into the implementation and coding specifics. As always, feel free to let me know your thoughts on the article. And seeing as a majority of this was written based on my own understanding of matters, please do correct me if I'm off anywhere!
item.author: jdprabasha

item.title: 用简单的术语解释编程 | Programming explained in simple terms
item.description:
编程 -它是一组分步指令，给计算机以执行我们希望它完成的特定任务。

计算机可以执行许多复杂的任务，例如复杂的计算、预订机票的软件、从附近餐馆在线订购食物等。

让我们在代码 IDE 中创建我们的第一个程序

输出将是Hello World

让我们一点一点地理解代码。 print("Hello, World");
 这称为Statement 。 每个程序都由语句组成，每个语句都有一个目标，即执行人类要求执行的操作。

函数 -此print()称为函数。 这些已经是编程语言中创建的术语，它的工作是在控制台上打印一些东西作为输出。

字符串 -这个" "被称为字符串。 在字符串中，您编写的任何文本都将按原样打印。 例如，如果您写了数字"9" ，那么数字 9 将按原样打印在控制台中。 所以字符串函数是按原样打印用户给出的任何输入。

括号 -这个()符号称为括号。 在 () 中，我们编写指令以在print等函数的帮助下执行。 必须在（）之间写指令来执行任务，如果不写代码将无法工作。

在许多程序中; 用来。

还有许多其他方法可以用不同的编程语言编写 print() 函数，例如printf() in C document.write() in JavaScript等。

通过点击❤来表达一些虚拟的爱。 😊 If you want to add something or want to suggest something, you can write in the comment section. 😊如果你想补充什么或者想建议什么，你可以写在评论部分。



原文:

Programming - It is a set of step by step instructions given to the computer to perform a certain task we want it to do.

The computer can perform many complex tasks like complex calculations, software for booking a ticket for a flight, ordering online food from nearby restaurants etc.

Let's create our first program in code IDE

The output will be Hello World

Let's understand code little by little. print("Hello, World");
 this is called a Statement. Every program is made up of statements, and each statement has a goal to do something which is told by a human to perform.

Function - This print() is called function. These are already created terms in programming language and its work is to print something on console as an output.

String - This " " is called a string. In the string whatever text you write will be printed as it is. for example, if you have written the number "9" then the number 9 will be printed in the console as it is. So string function is to print whatever input given by the user as it is.

Parentheses - This () symbol is called parentheses. In () we write instructions to perform with the help of functions like print. It is necessary to write instructions between () to perform the task, if not written then the code will not work.

; - This is used to end the statement of code. In many programs ; is used.

There are many other ways to write print() function in different programming languages like printf() in C, document.write() in JavaScript etc.

Show some virtual love by clicking ❤. 😊 If you want to add something or want to suggest something, you can write in the comment section.
item.author: prathmeshb

item.title: 我的全栈之旅🚀 | My Journey towards Full-Stack 🚀
item.description:
这一切是如何开始的？


在 2020 年大学的第一年，我们的大学俱乐部为我分配了一个简单的 Portfolio 项目，我必须使用基本的 HTML 和 CSS 创建该项目，但当时我只有 Android 手机。 我非常渴望学习和构建一些东西，幸运的是我从游戏商店找到了一个应用程序Sololearn并开始练习。 这对我来说是一次很棒的经历，因为我可以练习和渲染我的 HTML 和 CSS 文件。

我学习 JS 的下一步🥱
到目前为止，学习 JS 是我编程之旅中最艰难的步骤之一，因为我无法正确理解它，或者可能从未认真专注。 尝试从W3 Schools网站学习并学习了一些 Youtube、Coursera 课程，但是我创建了小型虚拟项目，但并不喜欢它们。 到目前为止，我一直在使用我的 android 手机进行网络开发。

我大学的明年📅
幸运的是，在第二年开始时，我有了笔记本电脑，但不幸的是，我对 Web 开发失去了兴趣，因为 JS 让我感到厌烦，我只是在浪费时间。 所以，然后我转向数据结构和算法（DSA），并用我的 C 编程知识解决了一些关于 Hackerrank 的问题。 它帮助我建立了解决问题的能力，我很喜欢它，但我仍然在想的一件事是学习发展。

我对 Python 很着迷


第 3 次 sem 即将结束，我开始探索 python。 现在我正在用 Python 解决 DSA 问题，并从事 2 个迷你有趣的项目。 我在 python 上度过了两个月，这些都很棒，但我仍然没有上网并在思考它。

与 Node.JS 相遇🔥🔥
我不知何故安装了 node 并在 Youtube 的帮助下学习了 express、MongoDB 并创建了实践项目，现在我的兴趣增加了并且我能够理解一些事情。

建立了我的梦想项目😴
为我的 Portfolio 的联系表单构建我自己的自定义后端是我一直想要的梦想功能之一。 我快速构建它并将其部署到 Heroku。 但是，有很多安全问题，但我可以成功构建它。 它仍在工作并且我没有更改任何代码行。

终于走上正轨了🛤️
毕竟，我对后端有了一个概述，然后我探索了 react 并构建了一堆项目，甚至还在构建中。 随着时间和需要，我学习了作为前端框架的 Tailwind CSS，React-query，为一个大学初创项目工作，并且再也没有回去过。

当前情景🤔
目前，我在第 6 学期，过去几个月一直在通过参加黑客马拉松来构建项目。 为什么要举办黑客马拉松？ 通过参加黑客马拉松，我有机会构建一些对他人有用的很酷的东西，并且需要在给定的时间范围内完成项目，这对于一段时间后学习新东西也是必要的。

结论✨
在这里，我们讨论了我的旅程，显然每个人都有自己的不同。 学习新东西需要时间，但你们都需要的是一致性。 我每天都在学习一些东西，我希望你能。

让我们通过Twitter联系并构建一些很酷的东西！😎



原文:

How It all started?


During first year of college in 2020 I was assigned a simple Portfolio project by our college club which I had to create using basic HTML & CSS but at that time I had my android phone only. I was too much eager to learn & build something, luckily I found a app Sololearn from the play store and started my practice over it. It was an awesome experience for me because I had something where I can practice & render my HTML and CSS file.

My Next Step Towards Learning JS🥱
Learning JS was one of the toughest steps in my programming journey till now because I was unable to understand it properly or maybe never focused seriously. Tried to learn from W3 Schools website & consumed a few Youtube, Coursera courses, however I created small dummy projects but didn't enjoy them. Up to that time I was using my android phone for web dev.

Next Year Of My College 📅
Luckily, at the start of the second year, I had my laptop but unfortunately, I had lost my interest in Web dev because JS bored me and I was just wasting my time. So, then I moved towards Data Structure & Algos(DSA) & solved a few questions over the Hackerrank with my C programming knowledge. It helped in building my problem-solving ability & I enjoyed it but one thing I had still in my mind was learning development.

I got crazy about Python


The 3rd sem was about to end & I had started exploring python. Now I was solving DSA problems with Python and working on 2 mini interesting projects. I spent two months with python & those were awesome but I was still out of the web & thinking about it.

Encounter with Node.JS🔥🔥
JS had bored me but up to that time I had heard a lot about Node "Node is a JavaScript runtime & blah blah...". I somehow installed node and learned express, MongoDB with the help of Youtube & created practice projects, now my interest had increased & I was able to understand things a little bit.

Built my dream project😴
Building my own custom backend for my Portfolio's contact form was one of the dream functionality I always wanted. I built it quickly & deployed it to the Heroku. However, there are a lot of security issues but I could successfully build it. It's still working & I haven't changed a single line of code.

Finally on the track🛤️
After all this I had an overview of the backend then I explored react & built a bunch of projects, even still building. With the time & need, I learned Tailwind CSS as a front-end framework, React-query, worked for a college start-up project & never went back.

Current Scenario 🤔
Currently, I'm in the 6th Semester and have been building projects for the last few months by participating in hackathons. Why hackathons? By participating in hackathons I have a chance to build something cool stuff which can be useful for others & need to complete the project in a given time frame that is also necessary to learn something new after a time.

Conclusion ✨
Here we discussed my journey obviously everyone have their different. It takes times to learn something new but what you all need is consistency. I'm learning something everyday, I hope you maybe.

Let's connect over the Twitter & build something cool!😎
item.author: gulshanaggarwal

item.title: 新手错误？ | Rookie Mistake?
item.description:
于是我冒了险……
自从我开始学习前端 Web 开发以来，我一直在跟踪其他菜鸟开发人员，看看他们的旅程和他们的作品集，我看到的一件事是，几乎每个“菜鸟”前端开发人员都有相同的项目他们的投资组合。 它总是一个天气应用程序、一个账单分割计算器或某种食物食谱应用程序——我不认为这有什么问题，但我想有点不同。 所以，从昨晚开始，我一直在想办法让我的作品集与常规作品有些不同。

今天早上它终于击中了我。 我有一个平台的想法，没什么太严肃的。 我已经完成了 UI/UX，我认为开发平台是明智的，所以我联系了一位经验丰富的全栈开发人员朋友，在审查我的代码时帮助我处理后端。

我对这个决定的问题是，我看到的几乎每一个作为开发人员开始的视频和博客文章，他们通常都反对承担完整的项目，总是从小事开始，慢慢进步。 我认为这没有什么问题，但我真的很想挑战自己去做不可能的事情。 我还不能在平台上提供完整的细节，但我会尝试分享我的进展，并希望能学到足够的知识/在我的投资组合中拥有一些独特的东西，以帮助我找到我作为前端开发人员的第一份工作。

感谢您的光临，敬请期待！



原文:

So I took a risk...
Since I started learning front-end web development, I have been stalking other noob developers to see their journey and also their portfolio, and one of the things I have seen is that almost every "noob" front-end developer have the same projects on their portfolio. It's always a weather app, a bill splitting calculator or some kind of food recipe app — I don't think there is anything wrong with this, but I'd like to be a little different. so, since last night I have been thinking of a way to make my portfolio a bit different from the norm.

It finally hit me this morning. I had an idea for a platform, nothing too serious. I had done the UI/UX and I thought it would be wise to develop the platform so I reached out to a friend who is an experienced full-stack developer to assist me with the backend while reviewing my codes.

The issue I have with this decision is that almost every video and blog post I have seen on starting as a developer, they are usually against taking on full projects, it's always to start with the little things and slowly progress. I don't see anything wrong with this but I'd really like to challenge myself to do the impossible. I cannot give full details on the platform yet, but I'll try to share my progress as I go, and hopefully learn enough / have something a little unique on my portfolio to help me land my first job as a front-end developer.

Thanks for coming and please, stay tuned!
item.author: soclarence

item.title: Appwrite 社区报告 #4 | Appwrite Community Report #4
item.description:
用于Appwrite的haimantika mitra

发表于大家好！ 来自 Appwrite 团队的问候👋

欢迎回到另一份每周社区报告，我们将在其中讨论过去一周 Appwrite 生态系统中发生的所有开源优点！ 这一次，我们有一些惊喜赠品，Appwrite x DEV Hackathon 进度更新、错误修复、新功能、使用 Appwrite 构建的 Google Docs 克隆等等！ 请继续关注到最后😉

把它带到 Appwrite hack 上！
  • 我们发布了一个与 Star Wars + Open Source 一致的秘密公告。 你能猜出它可能是什么吗？ 会是什么？ 它可能是错误的、有趣的，甚至是正确的！有资格获得🎁* 喜欢这篇文章

    敬请期待🤫

    我们听取您的反馈并尝试每周实施！

    以下是我们本周解决的问题：

    数组参数的默认限制，让 Appwrite 更安全

  • 用子查询替换 json 属性，使某些查询更快更安全。
  • 保存前通过 CLI 进行端点验证
  • 能够显示用户日志的运行时

    • 说说Appwrite的权限
    了解如何使用 Gordon Hayes 的 Appwrite 和 Flutter 创建 Google 文档克隆
  • 我们正在等待查看您构建的内容，请务必将它们放在这里。

    • Shabil Nedumpally - Shabil Nedumpally - Apr 28 4月28日



    原文:

    haimantika mitra for Appwrite

    Posted on Apr 30

    Hello everyone! Greetings from team Appwrite 👋

    Welcome back to yet another weekly community report where we talk about all the open source goodness that happened in the Appwrite ecosystem over the past week! 🤩

    TLDR; This time around we have some surprise giveaways, Appwrite x DEV Hackathon progress updates, bug fixes, new features, a Google Docs clone built with Appwrite, and a lot more! Stay tuned till the end 😉

    📢 What’s new:

    • Anom Chakravorty just built a snake game with Appwrite! 😮 Do you have a wacky wildcard solution too? Bring it on to Appwrite hack!
    • There’s a secret announcement we made that aligns with Star Wars + Open Source. Can you guess what it might be? 🤔
      🎁We are selecting a few random winners!🎁There’s some speculation with what our big news will be on May 4th. What will it be? An NFT? OSS related? 🤷‍♀️Take a guess! It can be wrong, funny, or even correct!To be eligible for a 🎁* Like this post

      * Comment with your guess

    P.S - We hear that there will be cool giveaways soon. Stay tuned 🤫

    ✅ Issues solved:

    We hear your feedback and try to implement them every week!

    Here are the issues we’ve fixed this week:

    ⚒️ What we’re currently working on:

    • Default limits on array parameters to make Appwrite more safe
    • Replacing json attributes with subqueries, to make some queries faster and safer.
    • Endpoint validation by CLI before saving
    • Runtimes to be able to display user logs

    🗣 Discussions:

    Let’s talk about Appwrite’s permissions
    🔗 https://github.com/appwrite/appwrite/discussions/2689

    📘 Resources:

    • Learn how to create a Google docs clone with Appwrite and Flutter by Gordon Hayes
    • 30 days of Appwrite is now updated, have you checked them out already?

      30 Days of Appwrite is a month long event focused at teaching you about all of Appwrite's core concepts and getting you ready to build production ready apps with Appwrite.

      We are awaiting to see what you build, be sure to drop them here .

    Shabil Nedumpally - Apr 28

item.title: 使用 Localazy 进行 Qt 本地化 | Qt localization with Localazy
item.description:
该框架在商业和开源许可下可用，并为程序员提供了许多易于使用的编程工具和预制的 GUI 元素。

在 GitHub 上查看本文的完整存储库。

它也可以在常用的 Linux 软件包存储库中找到。 它包含一个代码和 GUI 编辑器以及使整个编译过程相当简单和易于调试的各种工具。

打开Qt Creator后，新建一个项目（File - New File or Project），选择适合移动开发的Swipe模板为例。

现在，让我们将所有项目设置保留为默认值。 如果您为Android开发或其他目标平台配置了套件，您可以启用它们或稍后再启用。 也可以在初始项目设置向导中立即启用翻译，但我们稍后将手动添加它以涵盖整个过程，因为有人可能希望将翻译添加到已经存在的项目中。

主 qml 文件已经包含qsTr()函数中的一些字符串。 不是直接在属性中写入字符串，而是将其作为参数传递给根据活动语言对其进行翻译的函数。 然而，即使是原始字符串也可能会发生变化。 在这种情况下避免出现问题的一个好做法是使用 ID 而不是源语言字符串。 让我们使用qsTrId()代替qsTr()函数。 字符串参数应该是字符串的唯一 ID。 请注意，常用字符串可能具有相同的 ID。

在创建 ID 时遵循相同的模式是一种很好的做法，以便能够轻松识别给定字符串的所属位置。 已编译应用程序的编辑文件可能如下所示：

由于我们尚未提供翻译字符串，因此应用程序中的字符串也已更改为 ID。 首先，让我们为翻译创建一个目录，并在项目中添加预期的翻译文件。 编辑项目文件（ .pro扩展名）并添加翻译文件：

 RESOURCES += qml.qrc TRANSLATIONS = translations/EN.ts translations/CS.ts

现在定义了两个翻译文件，英语和捷克语。 在项目文件夹中手动创建翻译目录，然后只需使用工具 - 外部 - 语言学家 - 更新翻译 ( lupdate )。

外部工具 lupdate 生成翻译文件并在发生更改时更新它们。 可以通过右键单击qml.qrc文件并添加现有的翻译目录将文件添加到项目中。 然后文件应出现在项目文件树中。

此外，不要忘记在标题部分定义文件的语言。 以下是一份已完成翻译和一份未完成翻译的示例：

 ... <TS version="2.1" language="en_US"> ... <message id="page-1-header"> <location filename="../Page1Form.ui.qml" line="9"/> <source></source> <translation>Page 1</translation> </message> <message id="page-1-text"> <location filename="../Page1Form.ui.qml" line="15"/> <source></source> <translation type="unfinished"></translation> </message> ...

要查看结果并稍后通过 Localazy 轻松使用文件，应手动填写源语言文件。 源语言文件现已准备好，但尚未在应用程序中使用。 这将在main.cpp文件中完成。

必须创建、加载和安装一个新的 QTranslator 对象。 这是编辑后的main.cpp文件：

 #include <QGuiApplication> #include <QQmlApplicationEngine> //EDIT: include the header #include <QTranslator> int main(int argc, char *argv[]) { QGuiApplication app(argc, argv); QQmlApplicationEngine engine; const QUrl url(QStringLiteral("qrc:/main.qml")); QObject::connect(&engine, &QQmlApplicationEngine::objectCreated, &app, [url](QObject *obj, const QUrl &objUrl) { if (!obj && url == objUrl) QCoreApplication::exit(-1); }, Qt::QueuedConnection); //EDIT: create, load and install translator QTranslator translator; translator.load(":/translations/EN.qm"); app.installTranslator(&translator); engine.load(url); return app.exec(); }

嗯.qm文件？ 我们的项目中有这个吗？ 还没有！ 它们的生成方式与生成.ts文件时相同。

如果您不使用基于 ID 的方法，则不要使用更新翻译 ( lupdate )，而是使用发布翻译 ( lrelease ) 按钮。 但是， lrelease工具需要一个额外的参数，以防项目中存在 ID -idbased 。 可以从翻译目录执行一个简单的脚本或命令：

 /USE_YOUR_PATH_TO_QT/Qt/6.1.0/gcc_64/bin/lrelease ../QtLocalazy.pro -idbased

必须使用 Qt 安装的正确路径才能找到lrelease工具。 同样，在项目中添加新生成的.qm文件。 现在只需点击“运行”按钮，编译项目，然后……它就被翻译了！

创建 Localazy 帐户并安装或下载CLI 工具。 在 Localazy Web 界面的我的应用程序部分，点击添加新应用程序按钮，填写名称，然后选择源语言（在我们的例子中是英语）。

然后转到应用程序设置并获取读写访问密钥。 管理翻译的过程非常简单。 只需在翻译目录中创建一个新目录（以避免不必要的翻译覆盖）并根据文档添加配置文件。

在我们的例子中：

 { "writeKey": "your-write-key", "readKey": "your-read-key", "upload": { "type": "qt-ts", "files": "../EN.ts" }, "download": { "files": "./${lang}.ts" } }

然后调用localazy上传命令：

 PATH_TO_LOCALAZY_CLI/localazy upload

翻译现在应该作为源语言存在于 Localazy 引擎中。

点击添加语言按钮并创建新翻译（在我们的示例中为捷克语）后。 只需再次调用 CLI：

 PATH_TO_LOCALAZY_CLI/localazy download

一个新的cs.ts文件现在位于目录中。 只需将翻译目录中的CS.ts文件替换为下载的文件即可。 而不是EN.qm在翻译器中加载CS.qm ：

 translator.load(":/translations/CS.qm");

再次编译翻译（lrelease）并运行应用程序：

所描述的示例项目展示了如何与 Localazy 服务合作简单地添加和加载翻译。 为了能够在运行时动态更改语言，整个翻译器管理应该转移到一个独立的 C++ 类中，该类具有来自 QML 的可调用成员函数。

这样，可以加载多个翻译器，并根据选择的语言安装其中一个。 之后，无需重新启动应用程序即可删除活动的并由另一个替换。

本文由Nepanikar.eu开发团队的一名成员制作。 谢谢你。



原文:

Qt is a cross-platform toolkit for creating applications with a graphical user interface (GUI). This framework is available under commercial and open-source licences and provides the programmer with many programming tools and pre-made GUI elements that are easy to use.

Qt is commonly used not only for desktops but also for mobile application development.

See the full repository for this article at GitHub.

🆕 New project

Qt can be installed by directly downloading the package from Qt website. It is also available in the commonly used Linux package repositories. The main development tool provided in the Qt toolkit is called Qt Creator. It contains a code and GUI editor and various tools that are making the whole compilation process fairly simple and easy to debug.

After opening Qt Creator, let’s create a new project (File - New File or Project) and select, for example, the Swipe template which is suitable for mobile development.

🚩 Internationalization

For now, let’s leave all project settings at default values. If you have configured kits for Android development or other target platforms, you can enable them or do so later. It is also possible to enable the translation right away in the initial project settings wizard, but we will add it later manually to cover the whole process since someone might want to add the translations into an already existing project.

The main qml file already contains some strings in qsTr() function. Instead of writing the string directly in the property, it is passed as a parameter to the function that translates it according to the active language. Even the original strings, however, might change. A good practice to avoid problems in such cases is to use IDs instead of the source language strings. Let’s use qsTrId() instead of the qsTr() function. The string parameters should be unique IDs of the strings. Note that commonly used strings might have the same IDs.

It is a good practice to follow the same pattern when creating the IDs to be able to easily identify where does the given string belong. The edited files with the compiled application might then look like this:

The strings in the application have also changed to the IDs since we have not yet provided the translation strings. First of all, let’s create a directory for the translations and add the expected translation files in the project. Edit the project file (.pro extension) and add the translation files:

RESOURCES += qml.qrc
TRANSLATIONS = translations/EN.ts translations/CS.ts

Two translation files, English and Czech, are now defined. Manually create the translations directory in the project folder and then simply use Tools - External - Linguist - Update Translations (lupdate).

The external tool lupdate generates the translation files and updates them in case of changes. The files can be added to the project by right clicking the qml.qrc file and adding the existing translations directory. The files should then appear in the project file tree.

The .ts files are in XML format where each translation ID has one message element. Moreover, don’t forget to define the language of the file in the header part. Here is an example of one finished and one unfinished translation:

...
<TS version="2.1" language="en_US">
...
<message id="page-1-header">
    <location filename="../Page1Form.ui.qml" line="9"/>
    <source></source>
    <translation>Page 1</translation>
</message>
<message id="page-1-text">
    <location filename="../Page1Form.ui.qml" line="15"/>
    <source></source>
    <translation type="unfinished"></translation>
</message>
...

To see the result and to use the files easily later with Localazy, the source language file should be manually filled. The source language file is now prepared but it is not used in the application yet. This is to be done in the main.cpp file.

A new QTranslator object has to be created, loaded, and installed. Here is the edited main.cpp file:

#include <QGuiApplication>
#include <QQmlApplicationEngine>
//EDIT: include the header
#include <QTranslator> 

int main(int argc, char *argv[])
{
    QGuiApplication app(argc, argv);

    QQmlApplicationEngine engine;
    const QUrl url(QStringLiteral("qrc:/main.qml"));
    QObject::connect(&engine, &QQmlApplicationEngine::objectCreated,
                     &app, [url](QObject *obj, const QUrl &objUrl) {
        if (!obj && url == objUrl)
            QCoreApplication::exit(-1);
    }, Qt::QueuedConnection);

    //EDIT: create, load and install translator
    QTranslator translator;
    translator.load(":/translations/EN.qm");
    app.installTranslator(&translator);

    engine.load(url);
    return app.exec();
}

Hmm .qm file? Do we have that in our project? Not yet! The .qm files are the compiled translations that are used in the application. They can be generated in the same way as when generating the .ts files.

If you are not using the ID-based approach, then instead of Update Translations (lupdate) use the Release Translations (lrelease) button. The lrelease tool, however, needs an additional parameter in case of IDs present in the project -idbased. A simple script or command can be executed from the translations directory:

/USE_YOUR_PATH_TO_QT/Qt/6.1.0/gcc_64/bin/lrelease
../QtLocalazy.pro -idbased

The correct path to the Qt installation has to be used to find the lrelease tool. Again, add the newly generated .qm files in the project. Now simply hit the Run button, compile the project and… it’s translated!

❤️ Localazy

Create Localazy account and install or download the CLI tool. In My Apps section in the Localazy web interface hit the Add new App button, fill the name, and select the source language (English in our case).

Then go to the App settings and get the read and write access keys. The process of managing the translations is quite simple. Simply create a new directory in the translations one (to avoid unwanted overwriting of the translations) and add the configuration file according to the docs.

In our case:

{
  "writeKey": "your-write-key",
  "readKey": "your-read-key",

  "upload": {
    "type": "qt-ts",
    "files": "../EN.ts"
  },

  "download": {
    "files": "./${lang}.ts"
  } 
}

Then call the localazy upload command:

PATH_TO_LOCALAZY_CLI/localazy upload

The translation should be present in the Localazy engine now as the source language.

After hitting the Add language button and creating a new translation (Czech in our example). Simply call the CLI again:

PATH_TO_LOCALAZY_CLI/localazy download

A new cs.ts file is now in the directory. Simply replace the CS.ts file in the translations directory with the downloaded file. Instead of EN.qm load the CS.qm in the translator:

translator.load(":/translations/CS.qm");

Compile the translations again (lrelease) and run the application:

👍 Afterword

The described example project shows how to simply add and load a translation in cooperation with Localazy service. To be able to dynamically change the language in runtime, the whole translator management should be moved into a standalone C++ class with callable member functions from QML.

In such a way, multiple translators can be loaded and one of them can be installed according to the selected language. Later, the active one can be removed and replaced by another one without the need to restart the application.

This article has been produced by a member of the developer team at Nepanikar.eu. Thank you.
item.author: localazyteam

item.title: 什么是物联网，我们如何参与它的“精明”革命？ | What is the Internet of Things, and how can we participate in its "savvy" revolution?
item.description:
发表于你们可能都听说过物联网是第四次工业革命的关键部分......

近年来，物联网 (IoT) 领域呈爆炸式增长。
据研究，2018 年物联网技术的总成本为 6460亿美元。到 2022 年，预计将达到1 万亿美元。 一系列原因正在推动这种扩张。 一方面，

流行病导致对远程连接的需求增加。 另一方面，新的通信技术正在为开发人员和设备制造商开辟新的选择。

什么是物联网？

现在，谈谈基本的理解......
物联网或物联网是互连的计算设备、机械和数字机械、物品、动物和具有唯一标识符 (UID) 的人的网络，并且能够在不需要人与人或人对人的情况下传输数据- 计算机交互。

植入心脏监测器的人、带有生物芯片转发器的农场动物、带有内置传感器以在轮胎压力过低时向驾驶员发出警告的汽车，或任何其他可分配Internet 协议 (IP)的自然或人造物体地址和可以通过网络传输数据都是
物联网。

它如何改变生活，特别是“劳动力”。

尽管“物联网”(IoT) 听起来像是会说话的冰箱和自动启动汽车的未来浪潮，但相互交互的互联网连接小工具将对我们的生活产生超越“智能家居”的影响。 工人将从物联网中受益，因为它可以节省时间和金钱，同时也为发展和创新提供新的机会。

物联网 (IoT) 将成为数据处理强国。 这意味着企业将不得不重新思考他们如何收集和分析数据——决策者不仅需要学习和适应新型数据智能，而且物联网产生的数据量和种类也将引入新的或扩展的数据。数据分析师、战略家甚至客户服务的角色。

物联网提高生产力和效率的一种重要方式是使位置跟踪更加简单和无缝。 作为
目前在医院进行的联网设备和设备都将进行地理标记，这将节省工人寻找东西的时间，并通过降低丢失率来节省资金。

物联网 (IoT) 是您日常通勤中的下一件大事。 移动设备、汽车和您行驶的道路的互联性将帮助您通过减少旅行时间来节省时间，让您更快地上班或跑腿。

设备，但物联网还可以远程控制其他联网设备。

随着连接设备数量的增加，控制连接设备的复杂性也随之增加。 例如，智能手机现在被工人广泛用于交流、生产力和娱乐。 由于物联网，他们将能够操作物联网连接的设备以​​及执行其他任务。

不同的物联网领域需要不同的技能：

那些具有创造性思维和创造力的人想要一个
有趣的工作环境、专业成长和比 IT 专家选择物联网职业更高的薪水。 尽管对于有兴趣为技术发展做出贡献的学者来说，这是一个理想的话题，但应该注意的是，成为一名“深入的”物联网技术专家并不简单，因为了解一个平台或能力是不够的。

物联网开发人员需要开发由内而外的编程知识，以了解如何在服务器上收集、发送和存储数据。 了解 JavaScript 和 Python 等软件语言以及PHP、ASP.NET 或 Node.js等服务器端语言。 是必须的。

  • 与其他开发人员不同，在物联网领域工作的人必须对传感器和无线通信有深入的了解。 物联网信息分析、移动应用程序和 UI 开发的计算机科学背景确实有帮助。 对于物联网设备制造，电气、电子和仪器仪表工程方面的能力至关重要。

  • 除了计算之外，随着传感器收集物理数据，物联网将带您进入机械和土木工程的世界。

  • 数据科学和大数据管理的知识是一个额外的优势。 另一个重要方面是安全性。 缺乏数据安全是物联网应用在所有领域的最大威胁，而一些薪酬最高的工作是安全专业人员

物联网的职业机会

  • 数据科学家

  • 网络专业人士

  • 嵌入式程序工程师从 pcb 设计和制造开始，固件工程师，事物工程，完整的产品生命周期管理，机械工程师，标志调节，数字标志处理，互联网网关接口等。 每个学科都有广泛的物联网问题需要解决。

  • 风险识别、漏洞评估、公钥加密安全和无线社区安全的安全专家。

  • 传感器的研发设施需要设备和硬件专家，尤其是传感器和执行器方面的专家



原文:

Anushkaa27 for Bit By Bit Club

Posted on Apr 30

Y’all might have heard that the Internet of Things is a key part of the Fourth Industrial Revolution....

The Internet of Things (IoT) sector has exploded in recent years.
According to study, the total cost of IoT technology was $646 billion in 2018. In 2022, this is expected to reach $1 trillion. A slew of causes are driving this expansion. On the one hand, the

epidemic has resulted in an increase in the need for distant connectivity. New communication technologies, on the other hand, are opening up new options for developers and device makers.

What is IoT?

Now, talking about the basic understanding....
The internet of things, or IoT, is a network of interconnected computing devices, mechanical and digital machinery, items, animals, and people with unique identifiers (UIDs) and the ability to transfer data without requiring human-to-human or human-to-computer interaction.

A person with a heart monitor implant, a farm animal with a biochip transponder, a car with built-in sensors to warn the driver when tire pressure is low, or any other natural or manmade object that can be assigned an Internet Protocol (IP) address and can transfer data over a network are all examples of things in the
internet of things.

How is it transforming life specifically “workforce”.

Although the "Internet of Things" (IoT) may sound like a futuristic wave of talking refrigerators and self-starting vehicles, Internet-connected gadgets that interact with one another will have an impact on our lives beyond of the "smart home." Workers will benefit from IoT since it will save time and money while also providing new chances for development and innovation.

1. More data
The Internet of Things (IoT) will be a data processing powerhouse. This means that businesses will have to rethink how they collect and analyze data — not only will decision-makers need to learn and adapt to a new type of data intelligence, but the volume and variety of data generated by IoT will also introduce new or expanded roles for data analysts, strategists, and even customer service.

2. Know where everything is, all the time
One significant way IoT will increase productivity and efficiency is by making location tracking much simpler and more seamless. As
currently done in hospitals, Internet-connected equipment and devices will all be geographically tagged, which will save workers time hunting things down and save money by reducing the loss rate.

3. Get about more quickly
The Internet of Things (IoT) is the next big thing in your everyday commute. The interconnectedness of mobile devices, automobiles, and the road you drive on will help you save time by reducing travel time, allowing you to go to work faster or do errands faster.

4. Completely remote mobile device management (MDM) IT departments may have remote access to computers and mobile

devices, but IoT will also enable remote control of other Internet-connected devices.

5. Difficulty in managing devices has risen.
The complexity of controlling linked devices increases as the number of connected devices grows. Smartphones, for example, are now widely used by workers for communication, productivity, and pleasure. They will be able to operate IoT-connected devices as well as do other tasks as a result of IoT.

Various IoT domains need different skills:

Those with inventive thinking and creative qualities who want an
interesting work environment, professional growth, and greater salary than IT experts can choose a career in IoT. Though it is an ideal topic for academics interested in contributing to technological growth, it should be noted that becoming a 'deep' IoT technologist is not straightforward, as understanding one platform or competence is insufficient.

IoT developers need to develop an inside out knowledge of programming to understand how the data can be collected, sent and stored at the server. Knowing software languages as JavaScript and Python as well as server-side languages such as PHP, ASP.NET, or Node.js. is a must.

  • Unlike other developers, those who work in the IoT space must have a deep understanding of sensors and wireless communication. A background in computer science for IoT information analytics, mobile apps, and UI development does help. For IoT device manufacturing, competencies in electrical, electronics and instrumentation engineering would be essential.

  • Beyond computing, IoT will take you into the world of mechanical and civil engineering as sensors gather physics data.

  • Knowledge of data science and big data management comes as an added advantage. Another important aspect is security. Lack of data security is the greatest threat the Internet of Things applications in all domains and some of the highest paying jobs are of security professionals

Career opportunities in the Internet of Things

  • Data Scientists

  • Network professionals

  • Embedded Programs Engineer starting from pcb design & manufacture, firmware engineer, thing engineering, complete product lifestyles cycle management, mechanical engineers, sign conditioning, digital sign processing, internet gateway interfacing and so on. Each discipline has a totally wide range of troubles to resolve for IoT.

  • Security experts for risk identity, vulnerability evaluation, public key encryption security and wireless community security.

  • Device and Hardware experts, especially in Sensors and Actuators are required in the R&D facilities of sensor

item.title: 在 Python 中使用环境变量。 | Working with environment variables in Python.
item.description:
很多时候在开发软件时，我们发现需要读取其他软件定义的值，甚至自己定义这些变量！ 本文将讨论这一切。

访问全局环境变量

使用os.environ

我们可以使用os.environ字典获取和设置环境变量。 获取环境变量的值：

 >>> import os >>> os . environ [ "HOME" ] '/home/username'

设置环境变量的值：

 >>> import os >>> os . environ [ "FOO" ] = "bar" >>> os . environ [ "FOO" ] 'bar'

因为os.environ是一个mapping对象，我们可以使用.pop()从os.environ中删除一个变量：

 >>> import os >>> os . environ [ "FOO" ] = "bar" >>> print ( os . environ . get ( "FOO" )) bar >>> os . environ . pop ( "FOO" ) 'bar' >>> print ( os . environ . get ( "FOO" )) None

如果环境变量不存在，Python 将引发KeyError ，为了缓解这种情况，我们可以使用os.environ.get()如果环境变量不存在则返回None ：

 >>> import os >>> bar = os . environ . get ( "BAR" ) >>> print ( bar ) None

如果变量不存在，将返回此值。 我们可以通过传递默认值作为os.environ.get()的第二个参数来实现这一点

>>> import os >>> bar = os . environ . get ( "BAR" ) >>> print ( bar ) None >>> bar = os . environ . get ( "BAR" , "bar" ) >>> print ( bar ) bar

使用os.getenv()

现在您可能想知道：“ os.environ.get()和os.getenv()之间有什么区别？”，答案很简单，没有区别。 在性能方面，这两个功能的表现相对相似：

 $ python3 -m timeit -s "import os" "os.environ.get('HOME')" 500000 loops, best of 5: 730 nsec per loop $ python3 -m timeit -s "import os" "os.getenv('HOME')" 500000 loops, best of 5: 809 nsec per loop

访问.env文件中的变量

有时，我们想编写自己的环境变量而不干扰其他程序，无论是存储敏感数据还是只是配置选项，您都需要一个.env文件。

使用configparser访问文件

将configparser与 .env 文件一起使用的主要缺点是configparser需要节。 这意味着您的 .env 文件必须如下所示：

访问变量：

 >>> import configparser >>> config = configparser . ConfigParser () >>> config . read ( ".env" ) >>> var_1 = config [ "SECTION1" ][ "VAR_1" ] >>> print ( var_1 ) "foo"

使用python-dotenv访问文件

但是你也可以手动指定一个目录：

 ... load_dotenv ( "path/to/.env" ) ...

用例：管理敏感数据

假设您正在使用 API 令牌，并且不想将它们硬编码到您的代码中，并冒着意外将其推送到某些版本控制软件的风险。 您将使用环境变量来存储您的令牌。

 # .env TOKEN = abcdefg12345
 import os from dotenv import load_dotenv load_dotenv () token = os . getenv ( "TOKEN" ) # Do stuff with the token ...

结论

在本教程中，我们学习了如何使用os模块访问主机环境变量，以及如何使用configparser和python-dotenv访问存储在.env文件中的变量。

延伸阅读

  • 相关堆栈溢出问题
  • os.getenv
  • os.environ


原文:

Often times when developing software, we find the need to read values defined by other pieces of software, or even define these variables ourselves! This article will talk all about that.

Accessing global environment variables

Using os.environ

We can get and set environment variables using the os.environ dictionnary. Getting the value of an environment variable:

>>> import os
>>> os.environ["HOME"]
'/home/username'

Setting the value of an environment variable:

>>> import os
>>> os.environ["FOO"] = "bar"
>>> os.environ["FOO"]
'bar'

Because os.environ is a mapping object, we can use .pop() to delete a variable from os.environ:

>>> import os
>>> os.environ["FOO"] = "bar"
>>> print(os.environ.get("FOO"))
bar
>>> os.environ.pop("FOO")
'bar'
>>> print(os.environ.get("FOO"))
None

If the environment variable doesn't exist, Python will raise a KeyError, to mitigate this, we can use os.environ.get() which returns None if the environment variable doesn't exist:

>>> import os
>>> bar = os.environ.get("BAR")
>>> print(bar)
None

A neat trick of os.environ.get() is setting default values. This value will be returned if the variable doesn't exist. We can achieve this by passing the default value as the second parameter of os.environ.get()

>>> import os
>>> bar = os.environ.get("BAR")
>>> print(bar)
None
>>> bar = os.environ.get("BAR", "bar")
>>> print(bar)
bar

Using os.getenv()

Now you might be wondering: "What is the difference between os.environ.get() and os.getenv()?", well the answer is simply that there is no difference. In terms of performance, both functions perform relatively similarly:

$ python3 -m timeit -s "import os" "os.environ.get('HOME')"
500000 loops, best of 5: 730 nsec per loop
$  python3 -m timeit -s "import os" "os.getenv('HOME')"
500000 loops, best of 5: 809 nsec per loop

os.getenv's usage is quite similar to os.environ.get(), it has the same parameters and behavior:

>>> import os
>>> bar = os.getenv("BAR")
>>> print(bar)
None
>>> bar = os.getenv("BAR", "bar")
>>> print(bar)
bar

Accessing variables in a .env file

Sometimes, we would like to write our own environment variables without interfering with other programs, whether it's to store sensitive data or just configuration options, a .env file is what you need.

Accessing the file using configparser

The main drawback of using configparser with .env files is that configparser requires sections. Which means that your .env file will have to look like this:

Accessing the variables:

>>> import configparser
>>> config = configparser.ConfigParser()
>>> config.read(".env")
>>> var_1 = config["SECTION1"]["VAR_1"]
>>> print(var_1)
"foo"

Accessing the file using python-dotenv

python-dotenv is a Python library which allows us to read environment variables from a .env file using the os module:

>>> import os
>>> from dotenv import load_dotenv
>>>
>>> load_dotenv()
>>> foo = os.getenv("FOO")
>>> print(foo)
"bar"

load_dotenv() will search for a .env file in the current directory or parent directories by default, and then loads them to make them accessible by your project. But you can also manually specify a directory:

...
load_dotenv("path/to/.env")
...

Use case: Managing sensitive data

Let's say you were working with API tokens, and didn't want to hardcode them into your code, and risk accidentally pushing it to some version control software. You would use environment variables to store your token.

# .env
TOKEN=abcdefg12345

import os
from dotenv import load_dotenv load_dotenv()
token = os.getenv("TOKEN") # Do stuff with the token
...

Conclusion

In this tutorial we learned how to use the os module to access host environment variables, and also how to access variables stored in a .env file using configparser and python-dotenv.

Further Reading

  • Relevant Stack Overflow question
  • os.getenv
  • os.environ

item.title: 使用 SSR 在 Svelte Kit 中的多个客户端之间共享商店的状态 | Shared state of a store among multiple clients in Svelte Kit using SSR
item.description:
欢迎来到我的第一篇文章，我将在这里展示如何在多个客户之间共享一个苗条的商店，这意味着如果我有一个商店显示一个每 5 秒增加一次的计数器，那么所有客户都会看到相同的值柜台，无论他们何时打开我们的网站。
 当我们尝试向所有客户端提供相同的数据并避免重复的获取请求时，这很有用，以防我们有一个从 API 获取信息的数据存储，而不是计数器存储。

介绍

你好，在这篇文章中，我将描述我们如何拥有一个通用的 svelte 存储，它的状态在我们所有使用它的客户端之间共享Svelte-kit的load功能。

根据官方文档中的Load函数

改变服务器上的任何共享状态都会影响所有客户端，而不仅仅是当前客户端。

应用

所以，现在你可能想知道，这个的用例是什么？

让我们想象一下，在我们的index.svelte页面中，我们从一个将请求限制为 20 个请求/分钟的 API 获取前 10 只股票的价格。 如果我们有 3 个客户端同时打开我们的站点（30 个请求），第 3 个客户端将出现HTTP ERROR 429 （请求过多）。所有 3 个客户端的数据都是相同的，所以我们需要获取数据3倍？

好吧，如果我们的网站使用客户端渲染，是的，没有其他方法可以实现它，但是如果我们使用服务器端渲染呢？

当然，您可以让服务器运行并使用 Redis 或其他东西，但是如何在无服务器网站上“缓存”？

使用我向您介绍的方法，它可以保存商店的状态并在所有客户端之间共享，这样第一个客户端只完成一个请求。 这种状态将一直存在，直到您在setInterval中对其进行更新

所以，让我们看一些关于如何实现它的代码。

代码

下图描述了我们正在尝试的内容：

在此示例中，假设我们有一个公共存储，例如Counter store ，它将每 5 秒递增 1。

 // store.ts export const count = writable ( 0 ) export const dateNow = writable ( Date . now ()) //index.svelte < script context = " module " lang = " ts " > import type { Load } from ' @sveltejs/kit ' import { get } from ' svelte/store ' import { count , dateNow } from ' ../lib/store ' // we need hydrate to be false for this to work export const hydrate = false export const load : Load = async ({ fetch }) => { const FIVE_SEC_MS = 5 * 1000 setInterval (() => { if ( Date . now () - get ( dateNow ) > FIVE_SEC_MS ) { count . update (( n ) => n + 1 ) dateNow . update ( Date . now ) } }, FIVE_SEC_MS ) return {} } < /script > < script lang = " ts " > let countValue : number = 0 count . subscribe (( value ) => { countValue = value }) < /script > < span > { countValue } < /span >

我们有一个setInterval每 5 秒在后台运行一次，如果有新客户端， setInterval将被重建，导致我们试图避免的异步行为，无论如何都会发生这种情况，但是为了保持所有客户端同步，我们使用dateNow store这种方式只会发生 1 次同时更新。
然后我们检查(dateNow store - the current time)是否大于 5 秒，这样我们可以确保所有客户端保持同步。

你可以在这里找到源代码



原文:

Welcome to my first post, what I will show here is how to share a svelte store among multiple clients, that means if I have a store that displays a counter which is increased every 5 seconds, all the clients will see the same value of the counter, no matter when they opened our site.
That is useful when we attempt to feed the same data to all our clients and avoiding repetitive fetch requests, in case we have a data store that grabs info from an API, not a counter store.

Introduction

Hello, in this post I will describe how we can have a common svelte store that its state is shared among all our clients using it in the load function of Svelte-kit.

According to the Load function in the official docs

Mutating any shared state on the server will affect all clients, not just the current one.

Application

So, now you might be wondering, what is the use case for this?

Let's imagine that in our page index.svelte we are fetching the price of the top 10 stocks from an API that limits its requests to 20requests/min. If we have 3 clients opening our site at the same time (30 requests), the 3rd one will have an HTTP ERROR 429 (Too many requests).The data will be the same for all 3 clients, so do we need to fetch the data 3 times?

Well, if our site uses Client Side Rendering, yes, there is not another way to achieve it, but what if we use Server Side Rendering?

Of course, you could have a server running along and use Redis or something else, but how do you "cache" on a serverless website?

Using the approach I presented you, it sort of saves the state of the store and share it among all the clients, this way only 1 request is done by the first client. And this state will live until you update it in the setInterval

So, let's see some code on how to implement this.

Code

The following image describes what we are attempting to:

In this example let's suppose we have a common store e.g. a Counter store, which is gonna increment by 1 every 5 seconds.


// store.ts
export const count = writable(0)
export const dateNow = writable(Date.now()) //index.svelte
<script context="module" lang="ts"> import type { Load } from '@sveltejs/kit' import { get } from 'svelte/store' import { count, dateNow } from '../lib/store' // we need hydrate to be false for this to work export const hydrate = false export const load: Load = async ({ fetch }) => { const FIVE_SEC_MS = 5 * 1000 setInterval(() => { if (Date.now() - get(dateNow) > FIVE_SEC_MS) { count.update((n) => n + 1) dateNow.update(Date.now) } }, FIVE_SEC_MS) return {} }
</script>

<script lang="ts"> let countValue: number = 0 count.subscribe((value) => { countValue = value }) </script>

<span> {countValue}
</span>

We have a setInterval which is gonna be running on the background every 5 seconds, in case there is a new client, the setInterval is gonna be rebuilt causing asynchronous behavior we are trying to avoid, this will happen no matter what, but in order to keep all the clients synced we use the dateNow store this way only 1 simultaneous update will occur.
Then we check that the (dateNow store - the current time) is greater than 5 seconds, this way we make sure that we keep all clients synced.

You can find the source code here

item.title: 检查提高注意力的最实用技巧 | Check The Most Practical Tips To Improve Concentration
item.description:
在我们的整个学术生涯中，我们需要参加多项考试。 从小学的单元考试、半年考试和期末考试到大学的期末考试和竞争性考试。 虽然我们想尽最大努力在这些考试中获得最高分，但我们的注意力有时会让我们失望。 那么，提高注意力的最佳技巧是什么？

诸如引人入胜的电视节目、小说、与朋友外出以及几乎所有其他不学习的事情似乎都令人兴奋。 因此，这里有一些获胜方法，您可以在开始准备考试时使用它们来提高注意力。

注意：如果您认为考试是您学术课程中最重要的部分，那么您必须尝试一次我们的服务。 这里。 您将获得各种考试帮助在线服务。 立即与我们联系！

告别社交媒体

这是我们千禧一代最难做的事情。 现在，我们并不是要您将孩子完全排除在社交生活之外。 如果您有能力这样做，那将是一个绝妙的主意。 而且您可以确信，从长远来看，您的牺牲会得到回报。

但是，如果这听起来像是一项不可能完成的工作，那么请减少您在此类网站上花费的时间。 考试只是短暂的时间，但结果对我们的影响却是长远的。

尽可能多地做瑜伽

这也是提高注意力的最佳技巧之一。 瑜伽是我们的祖先给予人类的最伟大的礼物。 你有没有觉得你的脑袋被信息轰炸了？ 如果您的回答是肯定的，那么瑜伽练习是让您平静下来的最佳方式。

在背景中播放轻松的声音，只专注于您正在执行的瑜伽姿势。 只需几分钟，您就会感觉好多了，也更加放松了。 事实上，无论您是否正在为考试而学习，瑜伽本身都是一种奇妙的练习。 如果你练习一段时间，你就会明白我们在说什么！

分而治之

我们对工作的渴望与必须完成的课程量成反比。 许多学生在意识到为了通过考试而必须学习的广泛课程时会感到焦虑。

那么，在这种情况下，您有哪些选择？ 在开始编写教学大纲之前，您可以将其分解为更小的部分。

例如，如果您有很多时间，您可以将一章分解为 2-3 个子主题，并旨在每天从每个主题中涵盖其中一个。 通过这种方式，您可以同时涵盖所有主题，消除一天学习完整章节带来的紧张和单调。

每天看到小块的工作会让你保持热情，你的教学大纲会按时完成，不用担心太多。

做你认为适合你的事

这也是提高注意力的最佳技巧之一。 我们每个人都是独一无二的，对一个人来说简单的事情对另一个人来说可能很难。 这就是为什么在测试期间找出最适合您的方法是个好主意。 尝试复制其他人的方法绝不是一个好主意，因为这样做弊大于利。

创建适合您并获得结果的自己的计划。 例如，如果您白天生活在繁忙的氛围中，则可以在晚上学习。 如果您的同学在白天学习而您无法学习，请不要失望。

他们的环境可能会鼓励他们这样做，或者他们的方法对他们有用。 此外，为了获得好成绩，请尝试使您的环境适合考试。

在足够的水

您可能想知道我们的用水量如何与考试准备相关联。 让我们看看这是如何工作的。 水分别占我们大脑的 73% 和身体的 60%。 它有助于输送所有重要的营养物质，并为身体的各个部位提供电能，使它们发挥最佳功能。

此外，水不仅有助于清除体内毒素，还有助于大脑和注意力的正常工作。 当你准备考试时，最好在房间里放一个 2-3 升的水瓶，经常喝水，让自己重新焕发活力。

让我们结束讨论吧！

我们大多数人在生活中的某个阶段学习时都应对过考试压力和注意力下降的问题。 有时我们需要的只是有人告诉我们该做什么来提高我们的注意力。 因此，我们希望这些在测试期间提高注意力的技巧对您有所帮助。



原文:

Throughout our academic careers, we are required to take multiple tests. From unit tests, half-yearly, and final examinations in elementary school through semester-end exams in college and competitive exams. While we want to do our best and get the highest possible grade on these exams, our attention span occasionally fails us. So, what are the best tips to improve concentration?

Distractions such as engaging TV shows, novels, going out with friends, and just about everything else that isn't studying appears exciting. So, here are some of the winning methods that you may use to boost your attention when it's time to start studying for your exams.

Note: If you consider exams as the most important part of your academics session, then you must try our service once. Here. You will get all kinds of exam help online services. Get in touch with us now!

100% useful tips to improve concentration within minutes

Saying goodbye to social media

It is the most difficult thing for us millennials to do. Instagram, Facebook, Tumblr, and Twitter, which were previously your go-to sources of entertainment, need to be expelled from your life. Now, we're not asking you to exclude children from your social life entirely. If you are capable of doing so, it would be a fantastic idea. And you can be confident that your sacrifice will pay off in the long run.

However, if this sounds like an impossible effort, then cut down on the amount of time you spend on such sites. Exams are just for a short time, but the results have a long-term influence on us.

Do yoga as much as you can do

This is also one of the best tips to improve concentration. Yoga is the greatest gift that our ancestors have granted to humanity. Bakasana, Vrksasana, Vasisthasana, and Natarajasana are some of the exercises that might help with focus. Do you ever feel as though your head is bombed with information? If you answered yes, yoga exercises are the best way to calm your mind.

Play a relaxing sound in the background and focus only on the yoga position you're performing. You will feel a lot better and more relaxed in just a few minutes. In fact, whether or not you're studying for an exam, yoga is a fantastic practice in and of itself. If you practise it for a while, you'll understand what we're talking about!

Divide and rule

Our desire to work is inversely related to the amount of coursework that has to be completed. Many students become anxious when they realize the extensive curriculum that they must learn in order to pass their examinations.

So, what are your options in this scenario? Before you start working on your syllabus, you may break it down into smaller portions.

For example, if you have a lot of time, you may break down a chapter into 2-3 sub-topics and aim to cover one of them per day from each subject. In this manner, you may cover all of the subjects simultaneously, eliminating the tension and monotony that comes with studying a complete chapter in one day.

Seeing small pieces to work on every day will keep your enthusiasm going, and your syllabus will be completed on time and without too much worry.

Do what you find suit you

This is also one of the best tips to improve concentration. Each of us is unique, and what is simple for one person may be tough for another. That is why it is a good idea to figure out what works best for you during tests. It's never a good idea to try to replicate other people's methods since you'll do more harm than good.

Create your own plan that works for you and gets you results. For example, if you live in a busy atmosphere during the day, you can study at night. Don't be disappointed if your classmates study during the day while you are unable to do so.

Their environment may encourage them to do so, or their method works for them. Also, in order to get good results, attempt to make your environment exam-friendly.

During enough water

"If there is magic in our world, it is concentrated in water," Loren Eiseley correctly stated. You're probably wondering how our water consumption is linked to exam preparation. Let's look at how this works. Water makes up 73% of our brain and 60% of our body, respectively. It helps deliver all important nutrients and gives electrical energy to various regions of the body, allowing them to function at their best.

Furthermore, water not only helps in the removal of toxins from the body but also helps in the proper working of the brain and attention. When you are preparing for a test, it is a good idea to have a 2-3 liter water bottle in your room and drink from it frequently to re-energize yourself.

Let's finalize the discussion!

Most of us have dealt with exam stress and a waning attention span when studying at some point in our lives. Sometimes all we need is someone to tell us what to do in order to improve our concentration. So, we hope that these tips to improve concentration during tests have been of some use to you.

如果分数已经超过 21，则删除 11 并将其替换为 1。您可能需要查找 append() 和 remove()。

如果卡片中的 11 和 sum(cards) > 21：cards.remove(11)cards.append(1)

返回总和（卡）

如果计算机和用户的分数相同，则为平局。 如果计算机有二十一点 (0)，则用户输了。 如果用户有二十一点 (0)，则用户获胜。 如果 user_score 超过 21，那么用户就输了。 如果 computer_score 超过 21，则计算机失败。 如果以上都不是，那么得分最高的玩家获胜。 如果你和电脑都结束了，你就输了。

如果 user_score > 21 和 computer_score > 21：返回“你过去了。你输了😤” 如果 user_score == computer_score：返回“平局 🙃” elif computer_score == 0：返回“输，对手有二十一点😱” elif user_score == 0 ：返回“用二十一点赢😎” elif user_score > 21：返回“你赢了。你输了😭” elif computer_score > 21：返回“对手赢了。你赢了😁” elif user_score > computer_score：返回“你赢了😃”别的：

返回“你输了😤”

对于范围内的 _(2)：user_cards.append(deal_card())

虽然不是 is_game_over：
如果计算机或用户有二十一点 (0) 或用户的分数超过 21，则游戏结束。

 user_score = calculate_score(user_cards) computer_score = calculate_score(computer_cards) print(f" Your cards: {user_cards}, current score: {user_score}") print(f" Computer's first card: {computer_cards[0]}") if user_score == 0 or computer_score == 0 or user_score > 21: is_game_over = True else: --Hint 10: If the game has not ended, ask the user if they want to draw another card. If yes, then use the deal_card() function to add another card to the user_cards List. If no, then the game has ended. user_should_deal = input("Type 'y' to get another card, type 'n' to pass: ") if user_should_deal == "y": user_cards.append(deal_card()) else: is_game_over = True

只要分数低于 17，计算机就应该继续抽牌。

而 computer_score != 0 和 computer_score < 17: computer_cards.append(deal_card())

如果他们回答是，则清除控制台并开始新的二十一点游戏并显示来自 art.py 的徽标。

原文:

Mike Kameta

Posted on Apr 30

--Difficulty Normal 😎: Use all Hints below to complete the project. --Difficulty Hard 🤔: Use only Hints 1, 2, 3 to complete the project. --Difficulty Extra Hard 😭: Only use Hints 1 & 2 to complete the project.

--Difficulty Expert 🤯: Only use Hint 1 to complete the project.

######### Our Blackjack House Rules

--The deck is unlimited in size. -- There are no jokers. -- The Jack/Queen/King all count as 10. -- The the Ace can count as 11 or 1. -- Use the following list as the deck of cards: -- cards = [11, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 10, 10, 10] -- The cards in the list have equal probability of being drawn.

-- Cards are not removed from the deck as they are drawn.

-- ##################### Hints #####################

--Hint 1: Go to this website and try out the Blackjack game:
-- https://games.washingtonpost.com/games/blackjack/ --Then try out the completed Blackjack project here:

-- http://blackjack-final.appbrewery.repl.run

--Hint 2: Read this breakdown of program requirements:
-- http://listmoz.com/view/6h34DJpvJBFVRlZfJvxF
--Then try to create your own flowchart for the program.

--Hint 3: Download and read this flow chart I've created:
-- https://drive.google.com/uc?export=download&id=1rDkiHCrhaf9eX7u7yjM1qwSuyEk-rPnt

--Hint 4: Create a deal_card() function that uses the List below to return a random card. 11 is the Ace.

import random from replit import clear from art import logo print(logo)

print("Welcome to the game of Blackjack")

def deal_card(): """Returns a random card from the deck.""" cards = [11, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 10, 10, 10] card = random.choice(cards)

return card

--Hint 6: Create a function called calculate_score() that takes a List of cards as input and returns the score. Look up the sum() function to help you do this. def calculate_score(cards):

"""Take a list of cards and return the score calculated from the cards"""

--Hint 7: Inside calculate_score() check for a blackjack (a hand with only 2 cards: ace + 10) and return 0 instead of the actual score. 0 will represent a blackjack in our game.

if sum(cards) == 21 and len(cards) == 2: return 0

--Hint 8: Inside calculate_score() check for an 11 (ace). If the score is already over 21, remove the 11 and replace it with a 1. You might need to look up append() and remove().

if 11 in cards and sum(cards) > 21: cards.remove(11) cards.append(1)

return sum(cards)

--Hint 13: Create a function called compare() and pass in the user_score and computer_score. If the computer and user both have the same score, then it's a draw. If the computer has a blackjack (0), then the user loses. If the user has a blackjack (0), then the user wins. If the user_score is over 21, then the user loses. If the computer_score is over 21, then the computer loses. If none of the above, then the player with the highest score wins. def compare(user_score, computer_score):

--Bug fix. If you and the computer are both over, you lose.

if user_score > 21 and computer_score > 21: return "You went over. You lose 😤" if user_score == computer_score: return "Draw 🙃" elif computer_score == 0: return "Lose, opponent has Blackjack 😱" elif user_score == 0: return "Win with a Blackjack 😎" elif user_score > 21: return "You went over. You lose 😭" elif computer_score > 21: return "Opponent went over. You win 😁" elif user_score > computer_score: return "You win 😃" else:

return "You lose 😤"

def play_game():

--Hint 5: Deal the user and computer 2 cards each using

deal_card() user_cards = [] computer_cards = []

is_game_over = False

for _ in range(2): user_cards.append(deal_card())

computer_cards.append(deal_card())

--Hint 11: The score will need to be rechecked with every new card drawn and the checks in Hint 9 need to be repeated until the game ends.

while not is_game_over:
--Hint 9: Call calculate_score(). If the computer or the user has a blackjack (0) or if the user's score is over 21, then the game ends.

user_score = calculate_score(user_cards)
computer_score = calculate_score(computer_cards)
print(f"   Your cards: {user_cards}, current score: {user_score}")
print(f"   Computer's first card: {computer_cards[0]}")

if user_score == 0 or computer_score == 0 or user_score > 21:
  is_game_over = True
else:
  --Hint 10: If the game has not ended, ask the user if they want to draw another card. If yes, then use the deal_card() function to add another card to the user_cards List. If no, then the game has ended.

  user_should_deal = input("Type 'y' to get another card, type 'n' to pass: ")
  if user_should_deal == "y":
    user_cards.append(deal_card())
  else:
    is_game_over = True

--Hint 12: Once the user is done, it's time to let the computer play. The computer should keep drawing cards as long as it has a score less than 17.

while computer_score != 0 and computer_score < 17: computer_cards.append(deal_card())

computer_score = calculate_score(computer_cards)

print(f" Your final hand: {user_cards}, final score: {user_score}") print(f" Computer's final hand: {computer_cards}, final score: {computer_score}")

print(compare(user_score, computer_score))

--Hint 14: Ask the user if they want to restart the game. If they answer yes, clear the console and start a new game of blackjack and show the logo from art.py.

while input(f"Do you want to play a game? Type 'y' or 'n': ") == "y": clear()

play_game()

item.title: 你的架构好吗？ | Are you Well-Architected?
item.description:
在这篇文章中，我将尝试描述 AWS Well-Architected Framework 的重要性。 您可以在 AWS白皮书中找到更多详细信息。

每个架构师的目标都是创建健壮的软件系统。 创建良好的架构没有灵丹妙药，但无论采用何种技术，都有适用的最佳实践和原则。 该框架可帮助您在云中构建安全、可靠、高效、经济高效且可持续的工作负载。 它提供了一种方法，可以根据最佳实践持续衡量您的架构，确定需要改进的领域，解决缺点，从而在此过程中帮助您的架构不断发展。 该框架可以应用于各种云参与，例如云迁移、现有工作负载的重新设计和新工作负载。

框架

该框架包括一般设计原则、架构支柱、特定领域的镜头、动手实验室和架构良好的工具。

一般设计原则

该框架确定了一组通用设计原则，以在云中产生良好的架构：

  • 停止猜测您的容量需求。
  • 生产规模的测试系统。
  • 自动化使架构实验更容易。
  • 允许进化架构。
  • 使用数据驱动架构。
  • 通过比赛日提高。

六柱

架构完善的框架由六个支柱组成——卓越运营、安全性、可靠性、性能效率、成本优化和可持续性。 每个支柱都提供了一组设计原则、最佳实践和问题。 这些支柱可帮助您生产稳定高效的系统。

  1. 卓越运营：卓越运营支柱侧重于在云中运行和监控工作负载。
  2. 安全：安全支柱侧重于保护信息、系统和资产。
  3. 可靠性：可靠性支柱侧重于预防故障并从故障中快速恢复以满足需求的能力。
  4. 绩效效率：绩效效率支柱侧重于有效利用资源。
  5. 成本优化：成本优化支柱侧重于避免不必要的成本并以最低的价格提供业务价值。
  6. 可持续性：可持续性支柱侧重于最大限度地减少运行云工作负载的环境和社会影响。

我为 Well-Architected Framework 的六个支柱创建了一个思维导图，描述了每个支柱的设计原则、最佳实践和问题。 在此处查看思维导图。

镜头

在核心框架之上，还有一组针对特定领域的最佳实践和问题。

实验室

实验室为每个支柱提供初学者和高级水平的策划解决方案。

评价

理想情况下，进行架构审查是为了确定可以改进的领域，或者解决关键问题或关闭技术债务。 那么，您如何评估 AWS 上现有工作负载的健康状况？ 它为您提供了一个一致的机制来验证和衡量您的架构状态。 重要的是要注意审查过程不是审计； 相反，它应该被视为基于协作和对话的轻量级流程。 审查过程也不是一次性事件； 相反，它应该定期进行，例如在迁移的每个阶段或重要的工作负载里程碑。 审查过程的结果是一组必须关闭以改进工作负载架构的改进领域。

希望这篇文章可以帮助您了解 AWS 架构完善的框架如何指导您在 AWS 上构建架构完善的解决方案。



原文:

In this post, I will try to describe the significance of AWS Well-Architected Framework. You can find more details in AWS whitepaper.

Every architect aims at creating robust software systems. There is no silver bullet to creating good architecture but there are best practices and principles that apply regardless of the technology. AWS Well-Architected is a framework that provides set of guiding tenets to design and run successful solutions on cloud.

The framework helps you build secure, reliable, efficient, cost-effective, and sustainable workloads in the cloud. It provides a way to consistently measure your architecture against best practices, identify areas for improvement, address shortcomings and thus in the process helps your architecture to continuously evolve. The framework can be applied to all kinds of cloud engagements such as cloud migrations, re-design of existing workloads and new workloads.

The Framework

The AWS Well-Architected Framework describes design principles and architectural best practices for designing and running high quality solutions in the cloud. The framework includes general design principles, architecture pillars, domain-specific lenses, hands-on labs, and a well-architected tool.

General Design Principles

The framework identifies a set of general design principles to produce good architecture in the cloud:

  • Stop guessing your capacity needs.
  • Test systems at production scale.
  • Automate to make architectural experimentation easier.
  • Allow for evolutionary architectures.
  • Drive architectures using data.
  • Improve through game days.

Six Pillars

The Well-Architected Framework consists of six pillars - operational excellence, security, reliability, performance efficiency, cost optimization, and sustainability. Each pillar provides a set of design principles, best practices, and questions. These pillars help you produce stable and efficient systems.

  1. Operational Excellence: The operational excellence pillar focuses on running and monitoring workloads in the cloud.
  2. Security: The security pillar focuses on protecting information, systems and assets.
  3. Reliability: The reliability pillar focuses on the ability to prevent and quickly recover from failures to meet demands.
  4. Performance Efficiency: The performance efficiency pillar focuses on using resources efficiently.
  5. Cost optimization: The cost optimization pillar focuses on avoiding unneeded costs and delivering business value at the lowest price point.
  6. Sustainability: The sustainability pillar focuses on minimizing the environmental and societal impacts of running cloud workloads.

I created a mindmap for the six pillars of Well-Architected Framework depicting the design principles, best practices, and questions for each pillar. See the mindmap here.

The Lenses

AWS Well-Architected Lenses extend the guidance offered by AWS Well-Architected Framework to specific industry and technology domains, such as SaaS, Serverless, IoT, HPC, financial services, games industry and more. On top of the core framework, there is an added set of best practices and questions targeted for the specific domain.

The Labs

The AWS Well-Architected Labs provide you a repository of documentation and code to give you a hands-on experience in building a well-architected solution. The labs provide both beginner and advanced levels of curated solutions for each pillar.

The Review

Architecture reviews are ideally conducted to identify areas that could be improved, or to address critical issues or to close technical debts. So how do you assess the wellness of an existing workload on AWS? The AWS Well-Architected Tool helps you with the well-architected review process; it provides a consistent mechanism for you to validate and measure the state of your architecture. It is important to note that the review process is not an audit; instead it should be treated as a light-weight process that is based on collaboration and conversation. The review process is not a one time event either; instead it should be conducted on a periodic basis like at every phase of migration or at important workload milestones. The outcome of the review process is a set of improvement areas that must be closed to improve the workload architecture.

Hope this post helps you understand how AWS Well-Architected Framework guides you in building well-architected solutions on AWS.

item.title: React Redux Tutorial Part 2 -- counter アプリの incrementByAMount と incrementAsync を作成 | React Redux Tutorial Part 2 -- counter アプリの incrementByAMount と incrementAsync を作成
item.description:
const [ incrementAmount , setIncrementAmount ] = useState ( ' 2 ' );

増加量を useState を使って 2 で初化

< input aria - label = " Set increment amount " value = { incrementAmount } onChange = { e => setIncrementAmount ( e . target . value )} / >

増加量を変更できるロジックト UI を书き

< button onClick = {() => dispatch ( incrementByAmount ( Number ( incrementAmount ) || 0 )) } > Add Amount < /button >

クリックしたら増加量である incremenAmount の数だけ incrementByAmount が动き

incrementAmount に Number() を当てた値が 0 であれば増加量が 0 になるエラーハンドリングも当てる

これで、incrementByAmount は実装できた。



原文: 
 const [incrementAmount, setIncrementAmount] = useState('2');

増加量を useState を使って 2 で初期化

 <input aria-label="Set increment amount" value={incrementAmount} onChange={e => setIncrementAmount(e.target.value)} />

増加量を変更できるロジックと UI を書き

 <button onClick={() => dispatch(incrementByAmount(Number(incrementAmount) || 0)) } > Add Amount </button>

クリックしたら増加量である incremenAmount の数だけ incrementByAmount が動き

incrementAmount に Number() を当てた値が 0 であれば増加量が 0 になるエラーハンドリングも当てる

これで、 incrementByAmount は実装できた。

item.title: 使用 React Native 构建的 5 个令人难以置信的应用程序 | 5 Incredible Apps Built Using React Native
item.description:
它可以帮助您为您的企业创建高度 UI 设计和引人入胜的应用程序。

现在，开发人员正在移动设备上充分利用现代网络技术。

我们可以看到大量公司正在尝试使用该平台在应用商店进行应用开发。 无论是初创公司还是财富 500 强公司，React Native 都是每家希望在 Apple 应用商店和 Android 应用商店提交应用的公司的首选。

下面，我们列出了使用 React Native 进行应用程序开发的顶级公司。 您还可以在此平台上开发您的下一个应用程序项目，并聘请镇上最好的 React Native 应用程序开发服务。

彭博社

彭博是一家全球性的商业和金融新闻报道公司。 在 Bloomberg 的技术团队采用 React Native 进行移动应用程序开发之前，他们不得不花费大量时间在 iOS 和 android 上单独开发和更新他们的应用程序。 在对使用 react native 应用开发创建的原型进行彻底测试后，该平台被用于跨平台应用的各种更新。

民宿

当他们最初开始使用该框架时，他们意识到与现有的原生应用程序集成是昂贵的，但最终得到了回报。 根据在 AirBnB 工作的软件工程师的说法，每天大约有 60 名成员在使用 React Native。 代码的可重用性是使其从其他跨平台中脱颖而出的因素。

在 AirBnB，React Native 很容易掌握，但在开始做的过程中遇到了一些困难。 刚接触 React 的人在 React 应用程序的上下文中遇到了一些状态管理的想法，后来它变成了一个严重的问题。

脸书广告

动画和过渡完美无缺。

陀螺仪

它使用户能够查看他们的步数、心率、减肥、冥想、生产力等活动，以及更多使用 react native 为他们的应用程序。
可以在两个简单而全面的用户界面中跟踪整个数据：简单和卡片模式。 可以检索所有跟踪数据的每日、每周或每月报告，这有助于定义健康发展目标。

优食

尽管 React Native 涵盖了这个应用程序的一小部分，但它在 UI 和 UX 方面为用户增加了强大的体验。 该应用程序需要一个具有推送通知和声音通知等功能的仪表板。 使用 React Native 构建应用程序被证明是一个明智且成功的决定。 因为他们的团队之前已经有过使用 React 的经验。

阅读更多：如何增强 React Native 应用程序的性能

结束笔记

尽管该平台有许多相近的替代品（Flutter、Ionic）可用，但它继续显示出它的受欢迎程度。

其强大地位的原因之一是它得到了 JavaScript 的支持，一个庞大的 JavaScript 社区得到了它的支持。 每当 React Native 开发者陷入不希望的情况时，一个庞大的专家开发者社区总是可以提供相关的解决方案。

如果您正在考虑在跨平台上设计您的下一个应用程序，请选择 React Native 移动应用程序开发服务。 你可以查看我们的博客，比较React Native 与 Flutter 与 Ionic中的 React Native 替代方案。



原文:

React Native is an open-source cross-platform that serves as one of the best mobile app development platforms. It helps you create highly UI-designed and engaging apps for your business.

The evolution of React Native App Development Services is quite amazing. In 2013, what started as Facebook’s internal hackathon project is now one of the most used and trending frameworks around the world. In 2015, Facebook announced the availability of React Native on GitHub. Now, the developers are making the most of modern web technologies on mobile.

React native is the trending platform to build mobile app development. We can see a huge number of companies are trying to use this platform for their app development on app stores. Whether startups or Fortune 500 companies, React Native is a top choice of every company who wants to submit their app on the Apple app store and Android play store.

Below, we have listed the top companies that have used React Native for their app development. You can also develop your next app project on this platform, and hire the best react native app development services in town.

React Native App Examples

Bloomberg

Bloomberg is a global business and finance news reporting company. Before the technical team of Bloomberg adopted React Native for their mobile app development, they had to spend a massive amount of time on developing and updating their apps on iOS and android individually. After thorough testing of the prototype created with react native app development, this platform was adopted for various updation of the app for cross-platforms.

AirBnB

AirBnB is another famous brand that has its app built on react native. When they initially started using the framework, they recognized that integrating with existing native apps was pricey, but ultimately paid off. According to a software engineer working at AirBnB, there are around 60 members working on React native on a daily basis. The reusability of code is the factor that makes it stand out from other cross-platforms.

At AirBnB, it was easy to grasp react native, but there were some difficulties as they started working on it. People who were new to React struggled with some ideas of state management in the context of a React app, later it became a serious issue.

Facebook Ads

Facebook ads manager is the first full react native, cross-platform app built by Facebook. The JavaScript framework smoothly tackles the difference in ad formats, date formats, currencies, time zones, etc. This app has got simple navigation, a clean interface, and an intuitive UX that makes sure to give an amazing experience to users. The animation and transitions are flawless.

Gyroscope

Gyroscope is a popular fitness app built on react native development. It enables a user to see their step count, heart rate, activities like weight loss, meditation, productivity, and a lot more using react native for their app.
The entire data can be tracked in two simple yet comprehensive user interfaces: Simple and Cards mode. A daily, weekly, or monthly report can be retrieved for all the tracked data, that helps to define goals for health development.

UberEats

Although React Native covers a little part of this app, it adds a powerful experience to users in terms of UI and UX. The app needed a dashboard that have functions like push notifications, and sound notifications. Building an app using React Native turned out to be a smart and successful decision. As their team already had an experience with React previously.

Read More: How to Enhance the Performance of Your React Native App

Ending Notes

React Native mobile application development is one of the most used cross-platforms used globally today. Although many close alternatives (Flutter, Ionic) are available for this platform, it continues to show its popularity.

One of the reason for its strong standing is its backup by JavaScript, a huge community of JavaScript has its back. Whenever React Native Developers stuck in an unwanted situation, a huge community of expert developers is always available to provide the relevant solutions.

If you are considering to design your next app on cross-platform, go for react native mobile app development services. You can check out our blog on a comparison of alternatives of React Native at React Native vs Flutter vs Ionic.
