item.title: Dockerizing React 应用程序 | Dockerizing React App
item.description:
上野纯

发表于节点版本：v16.13.0
关联

这是 Dockerize React 应用程序的方法

目录

  1. 创建 React 应用
  2. 创建 Dockerfile、镜像并运行容器

米哈尔·诺科夫斯基—— Apr 29 4月29日



原文:

Jun Ueno

Posted on Apr 30

Node version: v16.13.0
Docker version: 20.10.12

Link

GitHub Repository
Here’s How to Dockerize React App

Table of Contents

  1. Create React app
  2. Create Dockerfile, image and run the container

1. Create React app

① Create React App following official tutorial ② Run command below

npx create-react-app <app name> # witch is going to be the folder name
cd <app name>
npm start

③ Access to http://localhost:3000 Confirm you can see React app welcome page

④ Stop Running by Ctrl + C

2. Create Dockerfile, image and run the container

① CreateDockerfile in root directory And copy the code below

# Dockerfile
FROM node:16
WORKDIR /reactapp
ENV PATH /reactapp/node_modules/.bin:$PATH
COPY package.json ./
COPY package-lock.json ./
RUN npm i
COPY . ./
CMD ["npm", "start"]

② Build image

docker build -t <image name> .

③ Run container

docker run \ -it \ --rm \ -v ${PWD}:/reactapp \ -v /reactapp/node_modules \ -p 3001:3000 \ -e CHOKIDAR_USEPOLLING=true \
    <image name>:latest

④ Access to http://localhost:3001 Confirm you can see React app welcome page

Michal Nowikowski - Apr 29
item.author: jun_uen0

item.title: 使用 React 和模块联合构建微前端 | Building Micro Frontend with React & Module Federation
item.description:
我们将使用 React、Redux、Typescript、Tailwind CSS、React Router 和 Webpack 创建一个可用于生产的微前端应用程序，但本文的范围过于广泛，将分成一个系列

在本单元中，我们将使用 React、Typescript 和 Tailwind CSS 设置一个简单的微前端应用程序，是的，为了简化事情，我们将使用 Lerna 设置一个单声道存储库。

什么是微前端？

微前端类似于微服务概念。 在这里，您看到的 WebApp 的每个部分，例如，标题可以在 react 中，侧边栏可以在 Angular、Vue 或任何其他框架中。 所以我们将有一个主机/容器应用程序，它将在加载时从不同的 URL 获取捆绑的代码。 它开辟了独立团队合作的可能性，没有任何相互依赖。

不要让你对细节感到厌烦，让我们开始吧，稍后会告诉你细节。

文件夹形成

以类似的方式创建文件夹。

 - micro-frontend - packages - header - host

是的，我们现在只是将标头用作微前端，主机将在加载时调用标头。 如果您想知道为什么我们在包下创建这些文件夹，那是因为我们使用的是 Lerna，这是推荐的做法。

标题

让我们在文件夹中初始化 npm。

现在安装主要依赖项。

模块联合尚未在 Create-React-App(CRA) 中实现。 所以将使用 webpack 5 来构建项目。 在 CRA 的底层，它使用 Webpack，但使用 CRA，我们完全摆脱了设置 webpack 的忙碌。 如果我们了解它在做什么，那么设置它并不复杂。

让我们安装开发依赖项。

 npm i -D @babel/core @babel/preset-react @babel/preset-typescript autoprefixer babel-loader css-loader file-loader html-webpack-plugin mini-css-extract-plugin postcss postcss-loader style-loader tailwindcss webpack webpack-cli webpack-dev-server

当我们使用 typescript 来编写这个项目时，让我们安装所需的类型定义。

 npm i -D @types/mini-css-extract-plugin @types/react @types/react-dom

现在，您的 package.json 将如下所示。

包.json

 { "name": "header", "version": "1.0.0", "description": "", "main": "index.js", "scripts": { "test": "echo \"Error: no test specified\" && exit 1" }, "author": "", "license": "ISC", "dependencies": { "react": "^18.0.0", "react-dom": "^18.0.0" }, "devDependencies": { "@babel/core": "^7.17.9", "@babel/preset-react": "^7.16.7", "@babel/preset-typescript": "^7.16.7", "@types/mini-css-extract-plugin": "^2.5.1", "@types/react": "^18.0.5", "@types/react-dom": "^18.0.1", "autoprefixer": "^10.4.4", "babel-loader": "^8.2.5", "css-loader": "^6.7.1", "file-loader": "^6.2.0", "html-webpack-plugin": "^5.5.0", "mini-css-extract-plugin": "^2.6.0", "postcss": "^8.4.12", "postcss-loader": "^6.2.1", "style-loader": "^3.3.1", "tailwindcss": "^3.0.24", "webpack": "^5.72.0", "webpack-cli": "^4.9.2", "webpack-dev-server": "^4.8.1" } }

如上所述，我们正在使用 webpack，为了限制本文的范围，我们不会详细介绍，而只是为您提供一个高级概述。

什么是 webpack？
在 webpack 中，我们有插件和模块，

加载器在文件级别工作，如果我们提到文件扩展名和依赖项，那么webpack将使用该依赖项来编译/转换具有提到的扩展名的文件。

插件在系统级别工作。 他们可以处理模式、文件系统处理（名称、路径）等。例如，我们正在使用 CleanWebpackPlugin，它将在生成另一个构建之前清理 bundle 文件夹。

它为每个包含 CSS 的 JS 文件创建一个 CSS 文件。 它支持 CSS 和 SourceMaps 的按需加载。

在下面，我们公开了 header 组件。

现在您知道什么是 webpack，让我们创建配置文件。

让我们创建反应文件。

这是因为您可能会遇到诸如Shared module is not available for eager consumption 之类的错误

索引.ts

引导程序.tsx

 import * as React from 'react'; import * as ReactDOM from 'react-dom'; import Header from './Header'; ReactDOM.render(<Header />, document.getElementById('root'));

头文件.tsx

 import * as React from 'react'; import "./header.scss" const Header = () => { return ( <nav class="font-sans flex flex-col text-center sm:flex-row sm:text-left sm:justify-between py-4 px-6 bg-white shadow sm:items-baseline w-full"> <div class="mb-2 sm:mb-0"> <a href="/home" class="text-2xl no-underline text-grey-darkest hover:text-blue-dark">Simple Header</a> </div> <div> <a href="/one" class="text-lg no-underline text-grey-darkest hover:text-blue-dark ml-4">Link 1</a> <a href="/two" class="text-lg no-underline text-grey-darkest hover:text-blue-dark ml-4">Link 2</a> <a href="/three" class="text-lg no-underline text-grey-darkest hover:text-blue-dark ml-4">Link 3</a> </div> </nav> ) }; export default Header

就是这样，现在如果你在这个文件夹中运行 npm serve ，它将开始在端口 3001 上运行

主持人

让我们创建主机应用程序并将标头应用程序调用到其中。

让我们启动 npm

和主要依赖

现在是开发依赖项。 如果您注意到，这里我们没有安装一些库，例如 Tailwind CSS，这不是必需的。

 npm i -D @babel/core @babel/preset-react @babel/preset-typescript babel-loader css-loader html-webpack-plugin mini-css-extract-plugin postcss postcss-loader style-loader webpack webpack-cli webpack-dev-server clean-webpack-plugin

现在您的 package.json 文件可能如下所示，不要错过将脚本部分添加到您的文件中。 运行应用程序需要它。

包.json

 { "name": "host", "version": "1.0.0", "description": "", "main": "index.js", "scripts": { "serve": "webpack serve --mode development --port 3000 --open", "build-dev": "webpack --mode development", "build-prod": "webpack --mode production" }, "author": "", "license": "ISC", "dependencies": { "clean-webpack-plugin": "^4.0.0", "react": "^18.0.0", "react-dom": "^18.0.0" }, "devDependencies": { "@babel/core": "^7.17.9", "@babel/preset-react": "^7.16.7", "@babel/preset-typescript": "^7.16.7", "babel-loader": "^8.2.5", "css-loader": "^6.7.1", "html-webpack-plugin": "^5.5.0", "mini-css-extract-plugin": "^2.6.0", "postcss": "^8.4.12", "postcss-loader": "^6.2.1", "style-loader": "^3.3.1", "webpack": "^5.72.0", "webpack-cli": "^4.9.2", "webpack-dev-server": "^4.8.1" } }

下面我们使用模块联合插件使用标头组件。

并让我们创建反应文件

索引.ts

引导程序.tsx

 import * as React from 'react'; import * as ReactDOM from 'react-dom'; import Host from './host'; ReactDOM.render(<Host />, document.getElementById('root'));

在这里，我们正在导入 header 并将其包装在 react suspense 中，因为我们延迟加载 header，它会显示一个指示器，直到所有子加载。

主机.tsx

 import * as React from 'react'; const Header = React.lazy(() => import('header/header')); const Host = () => ( <> <React.Suspense fallback="Loading..."> <div> <Header /> </div> </React.Suspense> </> ); export default Host;

在这里，我们需要标头的类型定义，因为实际的标头在我们通过 URL 获取的另一个项目中。

类型.d.ts

 declare module 'header/header' { export default Object }

现在，如果您在主机文件夹中运行 npm serve ，它将开始运行，并建议您在开始之前运行 header app ，否则它将只是空白

设置 Lerna 只是一个可选步骤，与微前端架构无关。 因此，如果您不想将所有内容都包含在单个存储库中，则可以跳过本节。

将以下文件复制到您的根文件夹（在您的包文件夹之外）并运行 npm 安装。

包.json

 { "name": "root", "private": true, "scripts": { "serve": "lerna run --parallel serve", "kill-ports": "kill-port --port 3000,3001,3002,3003,3004,3005,3006" }, "devDependencies": { "kill-port": "^1.6.1", "lerna": "^4.0.0" } }

并创建 lerna 配置文件。

够了！ 现在，如果您在根文件夹中运行 npm serve，Lerna 将开始并行启动每个应用程序。

GitHub Repo: https://github.com/blessonabraham/micro-frontend-react GitHub 回购： https ://github.com/blessonabraham/micro-frontend-react



原文:

We will be creating a production-ready micro-front end app using React, Redux, Typescript, Tailwind CSS, React Router, and Webpack but the scope of the article will be too broad and will be split up into a series

Here in this unit, we will be setting up a simple micro front end app with React, Typescript, and Tailwind CSS, and yes to simplify things, we will be using Lerna to set up a mono repo.

What's a Micro FrontEnd?

Micro frontends are similar to the microservices concept. Here each part of the WebApp you see, for instance, the Header can be in react and the sidebar can be in Angular, Vue, or any other framework. So we will have a Host/Container app that will fetch bundled codes from different URLs on load. It opens up the possibility of independent teamwork without any interdependency.

Without boring you much with the details, let's get started and will tell you the details later.

Folder Formation

Create the folders in a similar way.

- micro-frontend
   - packages
      - header
      - host

Yes, we will be just having a header as a micro front end for now, and the host will be calling the header on load. If you are wondering why we have created these folders under packages, it's because we are using Lerna, and it's the recommended practice.

Header

Lets initialize npm in the folder.

Now install the main dependencies.

Module federation is still not yet implemented in Create-React-App(CRA). So will be using webpack 5 to build the project. In CRA, under the hood, It's using Webpack but with CRA, we are completely freed up from the hustle of setting up webpack. It's not that complex to set it up if we understand what it's doing.

let's install the dev-dependencies.

npm i -D @babel/core @babel/preset-react @babel/preset-typescript autoprefixer babel-loader css-loader file-loader html-webpack-plugin mini-css-extract-plugin postcss postcss-loader style-loader tailwindcss webpack webpack-cli webpack-dev-server

As we are using typescript to write this project, let's install the required type definitions.

npm i -D @types/mini-css-extract-plugin @types/react @types/react-dom

Now, your package.json would look like the below.

package.json

{
  "name": "header",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "",
  "main": "index.js",
  "scripts": {
    "test": "echo \"Error: no test specified\" && exit 1"
  },
  "author": "",
  "license": "ISC",
  "dependencies": {
    "react": "^18.0.0",
    "react-dom": "^18.0.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@babel/core": "^7.17.9",
    "@babel/preset-react": "^7.16.7",
    "@babel/preset-typescript": "^7.16.7",
    "@types/mini-css-extract-plugin": "^2.5.1",
    "@types/react": "^18.0.5",
    "@types/react-dom": "^18.0.1",
    "autoprefixer": "^10.4.4",
    "babel-loader": "^8.2.5",
    "css-loader": "^6.7.1",
    "file-loader": "^6.2.0",
    "html-webpack-plugin": "^5.5.0",
    "mini-css-extract-plugin": "^2.6.0",
    "postcss": "^8.4.12",
    "postcss-loader": "^6.2.1",
    "style-loader": "^3.3.1",
    "tailwindcss": "^3.0.24",
    "webpack": "^5.72.0",
    "webpack-cli": "^4.9.2",
    "webpack-dev-server": "^4.8.1"
  }
}

As mentioned above, We are using webpack, to limit the scope of this article, we are not gonna go into many details but will just give you a high-level overview.

What is webpack?
Webpack is a module bundler library, Which means, that when we run an npm run/serve command against a webpack project, webpack will kick in and it will read through webpack.config.js then compile and build your project using the dependencies that we mentioned in this config file. In webpack we have plugins and modules,

Loaders work at a file level, If we mention the file extension and the dependency then webpack will use that dependency to compile/transpile the files with mentioned extensions.

Plugins work at a system level. They can work on the pattern, file system handling (name, path), etc. For instance, we are using CleanWebpackPlugin, which will clean the bundle folder before generating another build.

HtmlWebpackPlugin: It will generate an HTML5 file for you that includes all your webpack bundles in the body using script tags.

MiniCssExtractPlugin: It extracts CSS into separate files. It creates a CSS file per JS file which contains CSS. It supports On-Demand-Loading of CSS and SourceMaps.

ModuleFederationPlugin: Module federation allows a JavaScript application to dynamically run code from another bundle/build, on the client and server. And here below, we expose header component.

and now that you know what is webpack, let's create the config file.

webpack.config.js

const path = require('path')
const { CleanWebpackPlugin } = require('clean-webpack-plugin')
const HtmlWebpackPlugin = require('html-webpack-plugin')
const MiniCssExtractPlugin = require("mini-css-extract-plugin")
const ModuleFederationPlugin = require('webpack/lib/container/ModuleFederationPlugin');
const deps = require("./package.json").dependencies;

module.exports = {
    entry: './src/index.ts',
    output: {
        filename: '[name].[contenthash].js',
        path: path.join(process.cwd(), 'dist')
    },
    plugins: [
        new CleanWebpackPlugin(),
        new MiniCssExtractPlugin({
            filename: '[name].[contenthash].css',
        }),
        new HtmlWebpackPlugin({
            template: './public/index.html',
        }),
        new ModuleFederationPlugin({
            name: 'header',
            filename: 'remoteEntry.js',
            exposes: {
                './header': './src/Header',
            },
            shared: {
                ...deps,
                react: {
                    singleton: true,
                    requiredVersion: deps.react,
                },
                "react-dom": {
                    singleton: true,
                    requiredVersion: deps["react-dom"],
                },
            },
        })
    ],
    resolve: {
        extensions: ['.tsx', '.ts', '.js'],
    },
    module: {
        rules: [
            {
                test: /\.(ts|tsx)?$/,
                use: [{
                    loader: 'babel-loader',
                    options: {
                        presets: ["@babel/preset-typescript", "@babel/preset-react"]
                    }
                }],
                exclude: /[\\/]node_modules[\\/]/
            },
            {
                test: /\.(css|s[ac]ss)$/i,
                use: [MiniCssExtractPlugin.loader, 'css-loader', 'postcss-loader'],
            },
            {
                test: /\.(png|jpg|gif)$/i,
                type: 'asset/resource'
            }
        ]
    }
}

Let's create the react files.

index file, let's just import the Bootstrap file where we exactly do the stuff that usually gets done in the index file. Its because you might get run into an error like Shared module is not available for eager consumption

index.ts

bootstrap.tsx

import * as React from 'react';
import * as ReactDOM from 'react-dom';
import Header from './Header';

ReactDOM.render(<Header />, document.getElementById('root'));

header.tsx

import * as React from 'react';
import "./header.scss"

const Header = () => {
  return (
    <nav class="font-sans flex flex-col text-center sm:flex-row sm:text-left sm:justify-between py-4 px-6 bg-white shadow sm:items-baseline w-full">
      <div class="mb-2 sm:mb-0">
        <a href="/home" class="text-2xl no-underline text-grey-darkest hover:text-blue-dark">Simple Header</a>
      </div>
      <div>
        <a href="/one" class="text-lg no-underline text-grey-darkest hover:text-blue-dark ml-4">Link 1</a>
        <a href="/two" class="text-lg no-underline text-grey-darkest hover:text-blue-dark ml-4">Link 2</a>
        <a href="/three" class="text-lg no-underline text-grey-darkest hover:text-blue-dark ml-4">Link 3</a>
      </div>
    </nav>
  )
};
export default Header

header.css

@tailwind base;
@tailwind components;
@tailwind utilities;

that's it, now if you run npm serve in this folder, it will just start running on port 3001

Host

Let's create the host app and call the header app into it.

let's initiate npm

and the main dependencies

and now the dev-dependencies. if you notice, here we are not installing some libraries like Tailwind CSS, which is not necessary.

npm i -D @babel/core @babel/preset-react @babel/preset-typescript babel-loader css-loader html-webpack-plugin mini-css-extract-plugin postcss postcss-loader style-loader webpack webpack-cli webpack-dev-server clean-webpack-plugin

now your package.json file might look similar below, don't miss out to add the script section to yours. It's needed for running the app.

package.json

{
  "name": "host",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "",
  "main": "index.js",
  "scripts": {
    "serve": "webpack serve --mode development --port 3000  --open",
    "build-dev": "webpack --mode development",
    "build-prod": "webpack --mode production"
  },
  "author": "",
  "license": "ISC",
  "dependencies": {
    "clean-webpack-plugin": "^4.0.0",
    "react": "^18.0.0",
    "react-dom": "^18.0.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@babel/core": "^7.17.9",
    "@babel/preset-react": "^7.16.7",
    "@babel/preset-typescript": "^7.16.7",
    "babel-loader": "^8.2.5",
    "css-loader": "^6.7.1",
    "html-webpack-plugin": "^5.5.0",
    "mini-css-extract-plugin": "^2.6.0",
    "postcss": "^8.4.12",
    "postcss-loader": "^6.2.1",
    "style-loader": "^3.3.1",
    "webpack": "^5.72.0",
    "webpack-cli": "^4.9.2",
    "webpack-dev-server": "^4.8.1"
  }
}

And here below we consume the header component with the module federation plugin.

webpack.config.js

const path = require('path')
const { CleanWebpackPlugin } = require('clean-webpack-plugin')
const HtmlWebpackPlugin = require('html-webpack-plugin')
const MiniCssExtractPlugin = require("mini-css-extract-plugin")
const ModuleFederationPlugin = require('webpack/lib/container/ModuleFederationPlugin');

module.exports = {
    entry: './src/index.ts',
    output: {
        filename: '[name].[contenthash].js',
        path: path.join(process.cwd(), 'dist')
    },
    plugins: [
        new CleanWebpackPlugin(),
        new MiniCssExtractPlugin({
            filename: '[name].[contenthash].css',
        }),
        new HtmlWebpackPlugin({
            template: './public/index.html',
        }),
        new ModuleFederationPlugin({
            remotes: {
                header: 'header@http://localhost:3001/remoteEntry.js',
            }
        })
    ],
    resolve: {
        extensions: ['.tsx', '.ts', '.js'],
    },
    module: {
        rules: [
            {
                test: /\.(ts|tsx)?$/,
                use: [{
                    loader: 'babel-loader',
                    options: {
                        presets: ["@babel/preset-typescript", "@babel/preset-react"]
                    }
                }],
                exclude: /[\\/]node_modules[\\/]/
            }
        ]
    }
}

and lets create the react files

index.ts

bootstrap.tsx

import * as React from 'react';
import * as ReactDOM from 'react-dom';
import Host from './host';

ReactDOM.render(<Host />, document.getElementById('root'));

Here we are importing the header and wrapping it in react suspense because we are lazy loading the header and it will show an indicator till all the children load.

host.tsx

import * as React from 'react';
const Header = React.lazy(() => import('header/header'));

const Host = () => (
    <>
        <React.Suspense fallback="Loading...">
            <div>
                <Header />
            </div>
        </React.Suspense>
    </>
);

export default Host;

And here, we need the type definition for the header because the actual header is in another project which we are fetching via URL.

types.d.ts

declare module 'header/header' {
    export default Object
}

now at this point, if you run npm serve in the host folder, it will just start running and would suggest you run header app before starting this, or else it would be just blank

Monorepo - Setting up lerna

Setting up Lerna is just an optional step, which has nothing to do with micro front-end architecture. Mono-repo just helps us to run/serve all projects at once without going into each folder in our local system. So you can skip this section if you don't want to include everything in a single repo.

copy the below file to your root folder(outside of your package folder) and run an npm install.

package.json

{
  "name": "root",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "serve": "lerna run --parallel serve",
    "kill-ports": "kill-port --port 3000,3001,3002,3003,3004,3005,3006"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "kill-port": "^1.6.1",
    "lerna": "^4.0.0"
  }
}

and create the lerna config file.

lerna.json

{
    "packages": [
        "packages/*"
    ],
    "version": "0.0.0"
}

That's enough! Now, if you run an npm serve in the root folder Lerna will start launching each app in parallel.

GitHub Repo: https://github.com/blessonabraham/micro-frontend-react

原文:

The total number of digital global buyers in the world is increasing at a rapid rate. With online sales registering impressive growth figures, eCommerce websites have become an important business tool. Whether you have a business that you want to launch online or a traditional brick-and-mortar shop you want to expand, having a website is integral to its success.

You can now opt for the services of ORP Versatile MobiTech. From mobile app development to digital marketing, they will help you build a captivating and stunning website that will convert. Nowadays, a majority of eCommerce platforms are opting for a website development company in Houston to create engaging websites.

Apart from conversion, a user-centric website allows businesses to boost brand engagement. You can now experience exponential growth in revenue with the right eCommerce website. If you’re still wondering why it is crucial for eCommerce businesses to develop a website with an eCommerce website developer in Houston, here are the top reasons which will explain why.

What do your Customers want from Ecommerce Websites?

Here are some top things which your clients want from your eCommerce website.

  1. The website should have an easy and intuitive online shopping experience
  2. They always expect an aesthetically-pleasing website design
  3. Your clients always expect a highly personalized experience

With these things in mind, let’s take a glance at the top reasons why you should collaborate with an eCommerce development company.

***Leverage Domain-Specific Marketing Approach*

One of the key highlights of opting for an eCommerce marketing agency is to leverage a domain-specific marketing approach. In this manner, you can boost the overall engagement of your brand among the audiences.

With an eCommerce website, you can target your audiences based on age range, geographical location, and gender specifications. You should also execute Search Engine Optimization (SEO) strategies to bolster the popularity and visibility of your website.

24/7 Access and Shopping

You should consult ORP Versatile MobiTech to develop a compelling website for your eCommerce store. Note that your clients will expect you to operate 24/7. That’s why like your competitors, your eCommerce store should have a stunning website.

With a website, you can allow your users to place orders

irrespective of the time. Having an eCommerce website implies that your business doesn’t close when your staff members retire for the day. To develop a stunning and compelling website, opt for eCommerce website development services in Houston.

Allows you to market directly to the Customers

One of the major benefits of having an eCommerce website is that it will allow you to market directly to your customers. In other words, you’ll be able to eliminate any middlemen from the buying and selling process. Selling goods and services directly to the clients via the website will help you to provide a positive customer experience.

Furthermore, you will be able to gather customer-centric data which augurs well for your business prospects. Nowadays, a vast majority of businesses struggle to acquire repeated buyers when selling goods on leading marketplaces. An eCommerce website allows you to target your repeat buyers. To develop a user-centric eCommerce website, you should hire an eCommerce Web Design Company in Houston.

Boosts the Overall Reach and Popularity of your Brand

For an eCommerce store, it is important to boost the popularity of the company among the target audiences. If they fail to boost their brand’s overall reach and popularity, they will face a challenging time to sell their products.

This is exactly where an eCommerce website will help! With a thoughtfully designed website, you will provide your users with a wide variety of customizations. You can even provide them with highly customized and tailored shopping experiences that will cast an indelible imprint on them. Now you can strengthen your brand and boost its overall popularity with the help of web development services from ORP Versatile MobiTech.

Reach Globally

Irrespective of the country you are in, an eCommerce website developed by the top web and mobile App Development Company in Houston will help you to reach your global audiences. And there is no denying that global exposure will help you to get the most out of your marketing endeavors.

Your products and services have a high potential to grab the attention of the international market. With a good website, you’ll be able to reach global audiences. An eCommerce website provides your business with an identity on the web which fetches you rich dividends. ORP Versatile MobiTech, the best web Development Company in Houston, Texas will design a website by analyzing your business needs.

A vast majority of businesses will be able to save money by reducing the administrative costs in the presence of a website. Hence, a website for your eCommerce business is pretty integral to its overall success. You can leave imprints on your clients’ minds with an intricately designed eCommerce website.

Provide Detailed Product Information to your Customers

Lack of transparency is what deters your clients to shop from your online store. But you can address this issue effectively with the help of a stunning eCommerce website. To be precise, an eCommerce website allows you to provide detailed information about your products to the audience.

For instance, you can optimize your product pages and include relevant information about your offerings. You can even optimize the product pages based on the latest keywords to make them rank in Google and Bing search results.

item.title: 图像去噪 | Image denoising
item.description:
图像去噪使用图像的视觉内容（如颜色、纹理和形状）作为图像索引，从数据库中提取图像。 这些功能没有办法改变。 在这个设计中，我们提出了一种新的非锐化掩蔽系统，以增强图像的区别。

图像去噪是图像处理领域中一个深入研究的问题。 使用引入污泥去除噪音并进行相关分析。 该方法采用自适应标准头件控制跳动路径的捐赠，从而在高细节区域发生区分增强，并以噪声发现方式从图像去噪中传播混合噪声。

英格。 弗朗西斯科·奥多涅斯—— 编码美人鱼🧜‍♀️ - Apr 29 4月29日



原文:

The DENOISING is the fashion that is proposed in 1990. The thing of image denoising is to remove noise by differentiating it from the signal. Image DENOISING uses the visual content of images like color, texture, and shape as the image index to recoup the images from the database. These feature no way changed. In this design, we present a new system for un sharp masking for distinction enhancement of images.

Image denoising is a well-studied problem in the field of image processing. Use of introductory sludge to remove the noise and relative analysis b/ w them. The approach employs an adaptive standard headpiece controls the donation of the stropping path in such a way that distinction enhancement occurs in high detail areas and noise discovery fashion for spread mixed noise from Image Denoising.

Ing. Francisco Ordoñez - Apr 29

item.title: Microsoft Dynamics AX 升级 | Microsoft Dynamics AX Upgrade
item.description:
科尔康普滕茨

发表于虽然企业软件对于支持您不断变化的业务需求至关重要，但立即升级到最新版本是否始终符合您的最佳利益？ 不必要。 您理想的Dynamics AX 升级选项取决于您当前的软件版本如何满足您当前的需求。 评估以下四个主要考虑因素：

适合您业务的应用程序功能。 通过移动性、物联网、人工智能或其他新兴技术支持不断变化的需求。 当前的基础设施设置。

无论您的初始评估指向哪个方向，请考虑与知识渊博的 Microsoft Dynamics 合作伙伴交谈，他们可以帮助您对关键指标进行排名，以便进行更准确的财务和功能比较。

阅读以了解更多信息： Microsoft Dynamics AX 升级



原文:

Korcomptenz

Posted on Apr 30


While enterprise software is critical to supporting your evolving business needs, is it always in your best interest to immediately upgrade to the newest release? Not necessarily. Your ideal Dynamics AX upgrade options depend on how your current software version meets your current needs. Evaluate these four major considerations:

Microsoft support for your existing version. Application functionality for your business. Support for evolving needs – via mobility, IoT, AI, or other emerging technologies. Current infrastructure setup.

Whichever direction your initial assessment leads, consider speaking with a knowledgeable Microsoft Dynamics partner who can help you rank key metrics for a more accurate financial and functional comparison.

Read to Learn More: Microsoft Dynamics AX Upgrade

item.title: 曲线拟合：机器学习关键概念的解释 | Curve Fitting: An explain of key concepts of machine learning
item.description:
这篇文章通过多项式曲线拟合分析提出了一个简单的回归问题。 此外，还将解释机器学习的一些关键概念，如泛化、过拟合和模型选择。 数据生成

训练集和验证集都是综合生成的。 输入变量 X 由 [0, 1] 范围内的间隔均匀随机值生成。 输出（或目标数据）变量 y 由添加随机噪声的生成函数 sin(2πx) 生成。 封面图展示了目标数据（训练集）和 N = 10 个训练样例的生成函数的曲线。

多项式特征

首先，输入变量 X（表示单个特征）将根据以下函数转换为多项式特征 (X_poly)：

 def poly_features ( x , M ): N = len ( x ) x_poly = np . zeros ([ N , M + 1 ], dtype = np . float64 ) for i in range ( N ): for j in range ( M + 1 ): x_poly [ i , j ] = np . power ( x [ i ], j ) return x_poly

因此，大小为 N x 1 的列向量 X 将产生一个 N x M + 1 矩阵，其中 M 是多项式的阶。 例如，使用 M = 2 和 x = 0.3077 会产生如下三个特征：

下图展示了三个训练集示例的多项式特征。

训练

下一步旨在训练模型（使用训练数据），即找到乘以多项式特征的系数 W 允许我们进行预测 y_pred。 有一些优化算法，如梯度下降，可以最小化成本函数，在这种情况下，实际值 y 和预测值 y_pred 之间的误差。 这里使用正规方程来获得 W，如下面的方程（正规）给出：

其中X是N x M+1大小的多项式特征矩阵，y是N x 1大小的输出变量，W是M+1 x 1大小的系数。通过正规方程得到的系数由函数给出以下：

 def normal_equation ( x , y ): # Normal equation: w = ((x'*x)^-1) *x'*y xT = x . T w = np . dot ( np . dot ( np . linalg . pinv ( np . dot ( xT , x )), xT ), y ) return w

测量误差

有了系数 W，就需要测量真实值（输出变量 y）和预测值 y_pred 之间的误差。 它使用通过平方和误差（SSE）获得的均方根误差（RMSE）来评估训练模型的性能。 下面的方程得到误差：

其中 N 是示例的数量。 下面的函数计算这些误差：

 def error ( y , y_pred ): N = len ( y ) # Sum-of-squares error sse = np . sum ( np . power ( y_pred - y , 2 )) / 2 # Root mean squere error rmse = np . sqrt ( 2 * sse / N ) return sse , rmse

验证

下一步是评估验证集，这是模型训练中没有的数据。 这里验证模型是否适合验证集。 大多数步骤与训练相同，即如何获取多项式特征、进行预测和测量误差。

多项式阶次分析

下图显示了多项式的四个（M = 0, 1, 3, 9）不同阶数。 绿色曲线表示生成函数，蓝点表示目标数据（训练数据，X 和 y），红色曲线表示预测数据（y_pred）。 最差预测由 M = 0 表示，其次是 M = 1。M = 3 和 M = 9 是实现最佳预测的模型，即它们是最适合训练集的模型。

错误分析

分析 RMSE（在下图中），在 M = 0 和 M = 3 之间，训练和验证中的误差都有减小的趋势。但是对于 M = 9，人们注意到验证集中的误差要大得多，也就是说，随着多项式阶数的增加，模型预测训练集之外的示例变得更加困难。 总之，模型缺乏泛化能力。 多项式的阶数有助于选择模型，选择 M = 3，因为它是具有最小验证误差的模型。

防止过拟合的一种可能方法是添加更多示例，在这种情况下生成 N = 50 的训练和验证集，我们按多项式的顺序获得 RMSE，如下图所示：

因此，当添加更多示例时，可以防止 M = 9 模型的过度拟合。

完整的代码可在Github和Colab上找到。 如果帖子对您有帮助，请关注博客。
在Linkedin和Github上关注我。



原文:

This post presents a simple regression problem through Polynomial Curve Fitting analysis. Besides, will explain some key concepts of machine learning, as generalization, overfitting, and model selection. The Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning book of Christopher M. Bishop inspires the post.

Data generation

Both training and validation sets are synthetically generated. The input variable X is generated by spaced uniformly random values in range [0, 1]. The output (or target data) variable y is generated from the generating function sin(2πx) added of random noise. The figure in the cover presents the curves of the target data (training set) and the generating function of N = 10 training examples.

Polynomial features

First, the input variable X (that represents one single feature) will be transformed to polynomial features (X_poly), according to the below function:

def poly_features(x, M): N = len(x) x_poly = np.zeros([N, M+1], dtype=np.float64) for i in range(N): for j in range(M+1): x_poly[i, j] = np.power(x[i], j) return x_poly

Thus, the column vector X of size N x 1 will result in a N x M + 1 matrix, where M is the order of the polynomial. For example, using M = 2 and x = 0.3077 results in three features as follows:

The figure below presents the polynomial features of three examples of the training set.

Training

The next step aims to train the model (with training data), that is, find the coefficients W that multiplied by polynomial features allow us to make predictions y_pred. There are some optimization algorithms, like gradient descent, that minimize the cost function, in this case, the error between the real value y and the predicted value y_pred. Here is used the normal equation to obtain W, as given by the equation (normal) below:

Where X is the polynomial features matrix of size N x M+1, y is the output variable of size N x 1, and W is the coefficients of size M+1 x 1. The coefficients obtained through normal equation are given by the function below:

def normal_equation(x, y): # Normal equation: w = ((x'*x)^-1) *x'*y
 xT = x.T w = np.dot(np.dot(np.linalg.pinv(np.dot(xT, x)), xT), y) return w

Measuring the error

With the coefficients W in hand, it is necessary to measure the error between the real value (output variable y) and the predicted value y_pred. It is used the Root-Mean Square Error (RMSE) obtained through the Sum-of-Squares Error (SSE) to evaluate the performance of the training model. The equations below obtain the errors:

Where N is the number of examples. The functions below calculate these errors:

def error(y, y_pred): N = len(y) # Sum-of-squares error
 sse = np.sum(np.power(y_pred-y, 2))/2 # Root mean squere error
 rmse = np.sqrt(2*sse/N) return sse, rmse

Validation

The next step is to evaluate the validation set, which is data that was not in the model training. Here it is verified whether the model fits the validation set. Most steps are the same as training, how to get the polynomial features, make predictions and measure the error.

Polynomial order analysis

The figure below shows the four (M = 0, 1, 3, 9) different orders of the polynomial. The green curve represents the generating function, blue dots presents the target data (training data, X and y), and the red curve shows the predicted data (y_pred). The worst predictions are presented by M = 0, followed by M = 1. M = 3 and M = 9 are the ones who achieve the best prediction, that is, they are the models that best fit the training set.

Error analysis

Analyzing the RMSE (in the figure below), there is a tendency for the error to decrease in both training and validation between M = 0 and M = 3. But for M = 9, one notices a much bigger error in the validation set, that is, as the order of the polynomial grows it becomes more difficult for the model to predict examples outside the training set. In short, the model lacks the generalization ability. The behavior of M = 9 can be called overfitting.

The order of polynomial helps in selection model, which the choice is M = 3, as it is the model that has the smallest validation error.

One of the possible ways to prevent overfitting is to add more examples, in this case generating training and validation sets with N = 50, we obtain the RMSE by order of the polynomial as shown in the figure below:

Thus, when more examples are added, it is possible to prevent overfitting of the M = 9 model.

The complete code is available on Github and Colab. Follow the blog if the post it is helpful for you.
Follow me on Linkedin and Github.
item.author: rlrocha

item.title: 这<details>一个下拉菜单 | The <details> Of a Dropdown
item.description:
如何仅使用 HTML 和 CSS 构建漂亮的下拉面板

在 HTML 和 CSS 方面，我绝不会认为自己是专家。 但是当我尝试构建一个没有任何 JavaScript 的下拉面板时，我想我只是分享我发现对我来说非常有用的东西。

这是您可以根据需要使用和自定义的最小示例：

如果您想查看使用这种方法构建的更高级的下拉菜单并想了解这里发生了什么，请继续阅读。

有一个 HTML 标签可能是网站世界中鲜为人知但功能最强大的标签： <details>标签。

它通常与另一个使<details>标签大放异彩的标签一起出现： <summary>标签。

它基本上按照它所说的去做——它向你展示了一个摘要。 您可以单击它查看一些详细信息。 最好的功能是，它随每个浏览器开箱即用，无需 JavaScript 即可切换细节。 只是普通的旧 HTML。 这是默认情况下的样子：

哇，还没有引起太多的快乐。 但我们会到达那里。

让我们来调味吧🌶️

通过一些样式，上面的普通<details>和<summary>片段已经可以用于常见问题页面上的问题（摘要）和答案（详细信息）等令人兴奋的事情。

但让我们探索一些更令人兴奋的用例。 让我们构建一个下拉菜单，它可能存在于您网页的导航中。

首先，我们在<details>片段周围放置一个容器，该容器将代表下拉元素。 然后我们用汉堡菜单图标替换之前的摘要文本，并插入一个列表作为实际的下拉内容。 最后，我们用<nav>标记包裹整个下拉列表，并为所有这些提供一些快速的颜色样式。 等等：

隐藏披露小部件

让我们隐藏这个标记，让点击区域看起来更像一个通用按钮。

在 Firefox 中，我们可以通过设置list-style: none;来做到这一点。 我们的总结。 在其他浏览器中，您需要应用display: none; 对于摘要的伪类::-webkit-details-marker 。

我们还将即时修复<summary>的光标行为。 所以，这就是我们在应用这些修复后得到的结果：

分离细节

这可能已经很好地用作一个简单的下拉菜单。 但我们可以做得更好。

现在，当打开详细信息时，内容会放大详细信息容器，因此还会下推位于导航栏下方的任何其他内容。 在更复杂的导航中，我们可能希望有一个与触发摘要分离的下拉面板。

因此，接下来，我们将添加一些样式以在与位置无关的面板中显示实际菜单内容。

我们可以通过调整其位置属性来获得一个独立的菜单内容。 因此，我们将我们的内容包装到一个<div>容器中并给它一个绝对位置。 这样，打开的菜单内容现在直接显示在摘要下方，这是我们下拉菜单的打开触发器。

为了定义面板是在触发摘要的左侧还是右侧打开，我们应用了一个position: relative; 对于下拉

容器。 您现在可以通过应用right: 0;来自定义下拉面板的锚点。 或left: 0; 使用相对位置时，我们还需要确保下拉面板的最小内联尺寸与其最大内容宽度相比——否则我们的菜单项将出现不需要的换行符。

通过为下拉<div>容器提供额外的display: inline-block; 我们确保下拉面板仅在直接单击汉堡菜单图标时打开。
与之前类似，我们还添加了一些列表样式，使其看起来像一个菜单，并使其与导航和下面的文本内容更加不同：

关闭菜单

打开和关闭下拉菜单效果很好，它看起来像一个导航菜单。 但是，如果我们打开菜单并决定不单击其中的任何内容而是离开以与页面上的其他内容进行交互，仍然存在问题。 然后我们的菜单仍将是敞开的并涵盖底层内容。

所以，当我们点击菜单区域外的其他地方时，我们需要弄清楚如何再次关闭<details> 。

我们可以使用一个巧妙的小技巧来实现这种行为——同样不涉及任何 JavaScript。 当<details>容器处于打开状态时，单击任何<summary>内容都会隐藏菜单内容。

我们可以通过确保我们可以在菜单内容之外单击的唯一区域始终是<summary>区域来利用此行为。 因此，单击页面上任何其他位置总是会触发关闭菜单。

从技术上讲，这可以通过将摘要::before伪类扩大到完整视图大小来实现。 这是通过给它一个fixed的位置并将其扩展到所有四个视图角来完成的（将top 、 right 、 bottom和left设置为 0）。

为了填满整个屏幕，我们还需要设置::before伪类的content 。 默认情况下，详细内容显示的 z-index 高于相关摘要内容，因此我们不需要关心这一点。 您仍然可以与菜单内容进行交互。

下一个示例为summary::before的内容应用透明背景色，以便我们可以看到菜单内容后面覆盖整个屏幕的区域：

结论

这种方法可用于多种不同的下拉面板，包括导航菜单、共享小部件以及用于打开具有更多详细信息或操作的面板的各种按钮。

事实上，GitHub 对他们的克隆按钮和分支选择面板使用了类似的方法——这也是我从中获得灵感的地方，对所描述的方法进行了逆向工程：

通过一些小的添加和更多的 CSS 样式，我们可以将我们的示例变成一个闪亮的下拉菜单，就像您期望下拉菜单一样工作：

只要您不想将任何更复杂的交互放入下拉列表中，所描述的方法就不需要任何 JavaScript。

但是，如果您在单击下拉面板中的链接后没有重新加载页面，则需要添加一些 JavaScript 来切换<details>的打开状态。 这同样适用于下拉面板中应更改其打开状态的任何其他操作。

我希望你学到了一些关于如何构建下拉菜单的有用细节。 我们可以给出肯定的总结，即您可能并不总是需要 JavaScript 来构建交互式 Web 组件。

对于下拉菜单和下拉面板，它当然可以。


 这篇文章最初发布在我的个人博客上——包括复活节彩蛋；）
 致谢： Fabrizio Conti的标题图片。



原文:

How to build a beautiful dropdown panel with just HTML and CSS

I would by no means consider myself an expert when it comes to HTML & CSS. But I thought I just share what I found to work really well for me, when I tried to build a dropdown panel without any JavaScript.

tl;dr: You can build a fully functional dropdown panel by using plain HTML and CSS with no JavaScript involved by leveraging the <details> and <summary> tags. Here’s a minimal example that you can use and customize to your needs:

If you want to see more advanced dropdowns built with this approach and want to learn about what’s going on here, then read on.

There is an HTML tag that might count among the little known but most powerful tags in the world of websites: the <details> tag.

It usually appears together with another tag that makes the <details> tag shine: the <summary> tag.

It basically does what it says — it shows you a summary. And you can click on it to see some details. The best feature is, that it comes out of the box with each browser, no JavaScript needed for toggling the details. Just plain old HTML. Here’s what it looks like by default:

Wow, not sparking too much joy, yet. But we’ll get there.

Let’s Spice It Up 🌶️

With a bit of styling, the plain <details> and <summary> snippet from above can already be used for such exciting things as questions (summary) and answers (details) on your FAQ page.

But let us explore some even more exciting use cases. Let’s build a dropdown menu that might live in the navigation on your web page.

First, we put a container around our <details> snippet which will represent the dropdown element. Then we replace the former summary text with a hamburger menu icon and insert a list as the actual dropdown content. Last, we wrap the whole dropdown with a <nav> tag and give all of that some quick color styles. Et voilà:

Hiding The Disclosure Widget

The <details> tag comes with an open attribute and a disclosure widget (▶, ▼) which indicates whether the dropdown is opened and details are visible. Let’s hide this marker, so that the click area looks more like a general button.

In Firefox we can do so by setting list-style: none; for our summary. In other browsers you need to apply display: none; for the summary’s pseudo-class ::-webkit-details-marker.

We’ll also fix the cursor behavior for the <summary> on the fly. So, this is what we end up with after applying these fixes:

Detaching The Details

This might already serve well as a simple dropdown menu. But we can do much better.

Right now, when opening the details, the content enlarges the details container and therefore also pushes down any other content that lives below our navigation bar. In more complex navigations we might like to have a dropdown panel that is detached from the triggering summary.

So, next up, we’ll add some styles to display the actual menu content in a position-wise independent panel.

We can get an independent menu content by adjusting its position property. Hence, we wrap our content into a <div> container and give it an absolute position. With this, the opened menu content is now displayed directly below the summary, which is the opening trigger for our dropdown menu.

In order to define if the panel is opened to the left or the right of the triggering summary, we apply a position: relative; for the dropdown

container. You can now customize the dropdown panel’s anchor by applying right: 0; or left: 0; (which is the default) to the summary.

When using a relative position, we also need to make sure the dropdown panel has a minimum inline size of its maximum content width – else our menu items will have unwanted line breaks.

By giving the dropdown <div> container an additional display: inline-block; we make sure the dropdown panel only opens when clicking the hamburger menu icon directly.
Similar to before, we also add some list styles to make it already look like a menu and make it more distinct from the navigation and the text content below:

Closing The Menu

Opening and closing the dropdown works nicely and it looks like a navigation menu alright. However, there’s still an issue if we open our menu and decide to not click anything in it but leave for interacting with other content on the page. Then our menu will still be wide open and cover underlying content.

So, we need to figure out how to close the <details> again, whenever we click somewhere else outside the menu area.

We can use a neat little trick to reach this behavior — again without any JavaScript involved. When the <details> container is in the open state, then clicking any <summary> content will hide the menu content.

We can leverage this behavior by making sure, that the only area we can click on outside the menu content will always be the <summary> area. So, a click anywhere else on the page would always trigger closing the menu.

Technically this is possible by enlarging the summaries ::before pseudo-class to the full view size. This is done by giving it a fixed position and expanding it to all four view corners (setting top, right, bottom, and left to 0).

In order to fill the whole screen we also need to set the content of the ::before pseudo-class. By default the details content is displayed with a higher z-index than the related summary content, so we don’t need to care about this. You can still interact with the menu content.

The next example applies a transparent background color for the summary::before’s content, so that we can see the area covering the entire screen behind the menu content:

Conclusion

This approach can be used for a multitude of different dropdown panels, including navigation menus, sharing widgets, and all sorts of buttons that open a panel with further details or actions.

In fact, GitHub is using a similar approach for their clone button and the branch select panel — which is also where I took inspiration from to kind of reverse-engineer the described approach:

GitHub’s “Code” & “select branch” dropdown is built using <details> and <summary> tags

With some minor additions and a couple more CSS styles we can make our example into a shiny dropdown menu, that works just as you would expect a dropdown menu to work:

As long as you don’t want to put any more complex interactions into the dropdown, the described approach does not need any JavaScript.

However, if you don’t have a page reload after clicking a link in the dropdown panel, you would need to add some JavaScript to toggle the <details>s open state. The same applies for any additional actions from within the dropdown panel that should change its open state.

I hope you learned some useful details about how to built dropdowns. We can give the positive summary that you might not always need JavaScript to build interactive web components.

HTML and CSS might have you covered in more cases than you think. For dropdown menus and dropdown panels it certainly does.


This post was originally posted on my personal blog — Easter egg included ;)
https://paulgoetze.com/2020/05/09/the-details-of-a-dropdown


Credits: header image by Fabrizio Conti.
item.author: paulgoetze

item.title: 如何在 C# 中使用策略模式 | How to use the Strategy Pattern in C#
item.description:
策略模式是一种行为模式，由四人组定义的软件设计模式设计模式：可重用的面向对象软件的元素，它也在Head First Design Patterns中进行了讨论。

策略模式可以定义和封装许多算法，并使它们可以互换。 算法可能会独立变化，并且可能会提供不同的结果并启用不同的行为，但是它们都可以使用相同的方法签名来调用。

下图说明了如何实现策略模式的典型示例。

在策略模式中，可以在运行时更改类行为或其算法。 允许创建代表各种策略的对象和行为随其策略对象而变化的上下文对象。 策略对象改变上下文对象的执行算法。

策略模式的另一个名称是 *Policy*，在我看来，它为如何在软件应用程序中使用此模式提供了更好的线索。 企业软件开发中最常见的场景，可以在业务规则引擎中实现策略模式。

定义一系列算法，封装每个算法并使它们可互换。 策略让算法独立于使用它的客户而变化

设计模式

业务规则引擎 (BRE) 是一个应用程序，它使用预定义的逻辑来管理决策流程以确定结果。 这方面的一个例子可能是在银行贷款软件应用程序中，可以根据对问题的响应触发各种规则。 如果一个问题的答案是肯定的，或者另一个问题的答案是否定的，贷款批准的机会可能会受到影响，或者可能需要进一步的后续问题来进一步澄清财务信息。 这些规则有助于指导用户完成申请流程，并帮助金融机构确定资格并构建其业务。

我最近遇到了基本规则引擎设计模式，作者实际上只是描述了策略模式的典型实现，从他提供的类图中你可以看出，实现类似于上面的简化策略模式。

简化实施

在软件开发中，软件设计模式是针对常见问题的可重用解决方案。 软件设计模式是用于解决可在许多不同情况下使用的问题的描述或模板。

这本书收录了 23 种模式，这些模式使软件架构师能够创建灵活、优雅且最终可重用的设计模式，而无需为自己重新发现或重新发明设计解决方案。

通过garywoodfine.com上的一系列博客文章，我将讨论这些模式以及 C# .net 核心开发人员如何在跨平台 .net 核心软件解决方案中实现这些模式的更多信息。

软件设计模式

软件设计模式通常是……

在我们非常简单的策略模式实现中，我们将构建一个简单的控制台游戏，我们将邀请用户在终端中输入一个数字，并根据该值返回不同的值。 每个返回值将由不同的策略返回。

让我们首先为我们的策略声明一个接口，我们将其定义为Rule ，它将有两个方法Verify和Execute 。

 public interface IRule { bool Verify ( string choice ); void Execute (); }

本质上， Verfify方法将接收输入并根据输入确定策略是否可以执行，并向调用应用程序提供响应以表明是否适合执行策略。

让我们实现第一个实现接口的简单策略类。 我们将实施一个策略来确保用户提供了一个有效的数字作为输入，我们将我们的类命名为NotANumberRule.cs ，该类的代码如下。

 public class NotANumberRule : IRule { public bool Verify ( string choice ) { return ! choice . All ( char . IsDigit ); } public void Execute () { Console . WriteLine ( "We said enter a number " ); } }

我使我们的类的实现尽可能简单，以免分散策略模式的细节。 在验证方法中，我们遍历提供的字符串中的每个字符以确保它是数字，如果我们发现任何字母字符，那么我们希望 t 表示我们的策略应该被执行，因为我们想通知用户他们应该只输入一个数字。

我们可以创建任意数量的规则类，提供所有实现Rule接口。 在代码示例中，我实现了几个规则，每个规则都会在各种条件下触发。

我们现在可以实现我们的简单控制台应用程序，我们将在其中提示用户输入选择 1 到 10 之间的数字。

 class Program { static void Main ( string [] args ) { string val ; Console . Write ( "Enter a number between 1 & 10 : " ); val = Console . ReadLine (); }

我们现在需要再实现一段代码，它基本上将检索我们在应用程序中定义的所有规则，并根据条件确定要执行的规则。

我们使用一些反射来检索应用程序中的所有类型，并检查它们是否实现了IRule接口，如果实现了，我们将实例化该类并调用Verify方法以确定是否应该调用Execute方法。

我之前讨论过在 C# 中使用反射， 获取所有实现接口的 C# 类，下面的代码是稍微轻量级的实现。

 private static void ProcessInput ( string input ) { foreach ( var type in Assembly . GetExecutingAssembly (). GetTypes ()) { if (! typeof ( IRule ). IsAssignableFrom ( type ) || ! type . IsPublic || type . IsInterface ) continue ; var rule = ( IRule ) Assembly . GetExecutingAssembly (). CreateInstance ( type . FullName , false ); if ( rule . Verify ( input )) rule . Execute (); } }

现在有了这个方法，我们可以将它添加到我们的控制台应用程序中。

 static void Main ( string [] args ) { string val ; Console . Write ( "Enter a number between 1 & 10 : " ); val = Console . ReadLine (); ProcessInput ( val ); }

如果我们尝试运行应用程序并尝试输入任何不是数字的字符串，那么我们将激活我们的 NotANumber 规则。

让我们扩展我们的应用程序并实现另一个规则，该规则指定如果用户输入数字 2，则会生成随机的尖刻评论。

 public class SnarkyCommentRule : IRule { public bool Verify ( string choice ) { if (! choice . All ( char . IsDigit )) return false ; var answer = Convert . ToInt32 ( choice ); return answer == 2 ; } public void Execute () { var index = new Random (). Next ( responses . Count ); Console . WriteLine ( responses [ index ]); } private List < string > responses => new List < string >() { "To err is human to choose number 2 is plain stupid" , "Of all the numbers to choose you picked 2. Why?" , "A bird in the hand is worth 2 in the bush" , "You picked a couple of ones" , "Once bitten twice shy" , "An arch bishop Desmond" , "A vicar in a two two" , }; }

如果我们运行我们的应用程序并在终端中输入一个 2，我们现在应该会看到我们随机选择的响应之一显示。

结论

使用策略模式，可以在运行时更改类行为或算法。 为了实现策略模式，我们创建了代表各种策略的对象和一个其行为根据其策略对象而变化的上下文对象。

策略设计模式并不局限于某个应用领域。 相反，任何需要通过可变性、行为选项和更改来解决未决任务和问题的软件都是设计模式的主要候选者。



原文:

The Strategy Pattern is a Behavioral Pattern , software Design Patterns defined by the Gang of Four in Design Patterns: Elements of Reusable Object-Oriented Software , it is also discussed in Head First Design Patterns.

The Strategy pattern enables the definition and encapsulation of a number of algorithms and make them interchangeable. The algorithms may vary independently and may deliver different outcomes and enable different behaviour, however they can all be called using the same method signature.

A typical example of how the Strategy pattern is implemented is illustrated in the diagram below.

In Strategy pattern, a class behaviour or its algorithm can be changed at run time. Enabling the creation of objects which represent various strategies and a context object whose behaviour varies as per its strategy object. The strategy object changes the executing algorithm of the context object.

Another name for the Strategy pattern is *Policy*, which in my opinion provides a better clue on how this pattern can be utilised in software applications. The most common scenario in enterprise software development where the Strategy pattern can be implemented in a Business Rules Engine.

Define a family of algorithms, encapsulate each one and make them interchangeable. Strategy lets the algorithm vary independently from the clients that use it

Design Patterns

A business rules engine (BRE) is an application that manages decision processes using pre-defined logic to determine outcomes. BREs enable precise decision making, and are especially useful for complex dependencies, as well as in instances where regulatory or organisational rule changes frequently require logic changes.

An example of this may be in a Bank loan software application, there are various rules that can be triggered based on responses to questions. If the answer is yes to one question or no to another question, the chances of approval for a loan could be impacted or further subsequent questions may be required to further clarify financial information. These rules help guide users through the application process and help the financial institution determine the eligibility and structure their business.

I recently came across Basic Rules Engine Design Pattern, where in the author just actually describes the typical implementation of the Strategy pattern, as you’ll be able to tell from the class diagram he supplied, the implementation resembles the simplified Strategy Pattern above.

Simplified Implementation

In software development a Software Design Pattern is a reusable solution to commonly recurring problems. A software design pattern is a description or template used to solve a problem that can be used in many different situations.

In 1994, the so called Gang Of Four (GOF) published their book Design Patterns: Elements of Reusable Object-Oriented Software in which they presented a catalog of simple and succinct solutions to commonly occurring design problems.

The book captured 23 Patterns that enabled software architects to create flexible, elegant and ultimately reusable design patterns without having to rediscover or reinvent the design solutions for themselves.

Through a series of blog posts on garywoodfine.com I will discuss these patterns and more on how C# .net core developers can implement these patterns in cross platform .net core software solutions.

Software Design Patterns

Software Design patterns are typically…

In our very simple implementation of the strategy pattern we’ll build a simple console game, in which we’ll invite the user to enter a number in the terminal and based on the value we’ll return different values. Each of the return values will be returned by different strategies.

let’s start by declaring an interface for our strategies, which we’ll define as Rule which will have two methods a Verify and an Execute.

public interface IRule { bool Verify(string choice); void Execute(); }

Essentially the Verfify method will taken in the input and determine if the strategy can be executed based on the input and provide a response back to calling application to signify whether it is appropriate to execute the strategy.

The Execute will enable the calling application to execute the strategy.

Lets implement a first simple strategy class implementing the interface. We’ll implementing a strategy to ensure the user has provided a valid number as input, We’ll name our class NotANumberRule.cs the code for the class will be as follows.

public class NotANumberRule : IRule { public bool Verify(string choice) { return !choice.All(char.IsDigit); } public void Execute() { Console.WriteLine("We said enter a number "); } }

I’ve kept the implementation of our class as simple as possible as not to distract from the detail of the strategy pattern. In the Verify method we iterate through each char in the supplied string to ensure that it is a Numeric digit, if we find any alpha characters then we want t signify that our strategy should be executed because we want to notify the user that they should only enter a number.

We can create any number of Rules classes providing the all implement the Rule interface. In the code sample I have implemented several rules each will trigger on various conditions.

We can now implement our simple Console application, in which we’ll prompt the user to enter to choose a number between 1 and 10.

class Program { static void Main(string[] args) { string val; Console.Write("Enter a number between 1 & 10 : "); val = Console.ReadLine(); }

We now need to implement one more bit of code, which will basically retrieve all our Rules that we have defined in our application and determine which rule to execute based on the conditions.

We use a some Reflection to retrieve all types in our application and check to see if they implement the IRule interface, if they do we will instantiate the class and call the Verify method to determine if we should call the Execute method.

I have previously discussed using reflection in C# ,Get All C# Classes Implementing an Interface , the code below is slightly more lightweight implementation.

private static void ProcessInput(string input) { foreach (var type in Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly().GetTypes()) { if (!typeof(IRule).IsAssignableFrom(type) || !type.IsPublic || type.IsInterface) continue; var rule = (IRule)Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly().CreateInstance(type.FullName, false); if (rule.Verify(input)) rule.Execute(); } }

With this method now in place we can add it to our Console application.

static void Main(string[] args)
{ string val; Console.Write("Enter a number between 1 & 10 : "); val = Console.ReadLine(); ProcessInput(val);
}

If we attempt to run the application and attempt to enter any string that is not a number then we’ll activate our NotANumber rule.

Let’s extend our application and implement another rule, which specifies if the user enters a number 2, that a random snarky comment is generated.

 public class SnarkyCommentRule : IRule { public bool Verify(string choice) { if (!choice.All(char.IsDigit)) return false; var answer = Convert.ToInt32(choice); return answer == 2; } public void Execute() { var index = new Random().Next(responses.Count); Console.WriteLine(responses[index]); } private List<string> responses => new List<string>() { "To err is human to choose number 2 is plain stupid", "Of all the numbers to choose you picked 2. Why?", "A bird in the hand is worth 2 in the bush", "You picked a couple of ones", "Once bitten twice shy", "An arch bishop Desmond", "A vicar in a two two", };
}

if we run our application and enter a two in the terminal we should now see one of our randomly selected responses displayed.

Conclusion

Using the Strategy pattern, a class behaviour or algorithm can be changed at run time. To implement the Strategy pattern, we create objects which represent various strategies and a context object whose behaviour varies as per its strategy object.

The strategy design pattern is not limited to a certain area of application. Instead, Any software that needs to solve pending tasks and problems with variability, behaviour options, and changes is a prime candidate for the design pattern.
item.author: gary_woodfine

item.title: 怀疑 | Suspicion
item.description:
对于昨天关于信任的帖子，我已经有了一些深思熟虑的回应。 我用一个请求结束了我的想法：

如果有人能解释为什么他们避免使用本机浏览器功能但使用第三方代码，我会很高兴。

克里斯有义务：

我不能代表这个行业，但我有一个猜测。 第三方代码（如引用的 Bootstrap 和 React）在解决重大跨浏览器问题和提供优于浏览器的人体工程学 API 方面有着悠久的历史。 创建 jQuery 是为了解决跨浏览器的 JavaScript 问题。 那是信任。

非常真实！ 但是 jQuery 也是我们不再需要的库的典型示例，因为浏览器已经赶上了……而且那些浏览器支持直接受 jQuery 影响的标准。 这是图书馆的成功故事！

查尔斯哈里斯在他的关于浏览器功能的库中回答了我的问题：

我认为这种信任的观点在过去可能就像 5 年的 JavaScript 开发几乎完全基于浏览器功能支持的不平等一样，已经在开发人员中受到了影响。 但就在 2019 年， 5 个顶级 Web 开发人员需求中有 4 个与浏览器兼容性有关。

浏览器兼容性是库（尤其是 Jeremy 引用的大型库，如 React 和 Bootstrap）向开发人员做出的基本承诺之一。

再说一次，是浏览器的不兼容性使库具有吸引力。

我们不信任浏览器，因为我们受过训练。 多年来与图书馆填补空白的浏览器缺陷作斗争。 浏览器敌人； 图书馆的朋友。

例如：jQuery 为规范跨浏览器的工作创造了奇迹。 编写一次代码，在任何浏览器中运行它——自信地。

三对三。 我的问题已经得到解答：人们倾向于图书馆，因为浏览器的实现不一致。

我故意在那里使用过去时。 当 Jim 说我们受过训练，不相信浏览器在支持标准方面具有同等地位时，我认为他是在做某事。 但这已经改变了。

查尔斯再次：

这种方法不是一种可持续的做法，我正在尝试尽可能少地这样做。 跨浏览器兼容性变得更好， Interop 2022等活动在减轻负担方面做了很多工作。 情况正在好转，但那种愤怒的“我只想上班”的心态很难消除。

我同意。 惯性是一种强大的力量。 无论跨浏览器兼容性有多好，开发人员都需要很长时间才能摆脱怀疑。

吉姆是半杯半满的人：

我对浏览器原生功能和 API 的信任正在恢复感到乐观。

当涉及到第三方库和框架时，他还指出了一种非常明智的心态：

从这个意义上说，第三方代码和抽象可以成为 Web 平台的出色 polyfill。 这个想法是默认的姿势应该是：尽可能多地利用 Web 平台，然后在创建出色用户体验方面存在差距，用探索性库或框架功能（可以想象，有朝一日可能成为本机在浏览器中）。

是的！ 一种使用第三方代码的渐进增强方法很有意义。 我一直认为你应该像对待牛一样对待库和框架，而不是宠物。 不要太执着。 如果该库正在解决真正的需求，它将被浏览器中稳定的 Web 标准所取代（再次参见 jQuery）。

我认为第三方库和框架作为 polyfill 效果最好。 但是 polyfill 的全部意义在于，只有在浏览器本身不提供功能时才使用它们（当浏览器支持该功能时，您也可以稍后返回并删除 polyfill）。 但这不是人们今天使用库和框架的方式。 开发人员默认使用它们，而不是将它们视为最后的手段。

我喜欢 Jim 提出的设计原则：

在可用的情况下，默认使用浏览器原生功能而不是第三方代码、抽象或惯用语。

(PS It's kind of lovely to see this kind of thoughtful blog-to-blog conversation happening. Right at a time when Twitter is about to go down the tubes, this is a demonstration of an actual public square with more nuanced discussion. Make your own website and join the conversation!) （PS 看到这种深思熟虑的博客到博客的对话真是太好了。就在 Twitter 即将下线的时候，这是一个实际的公共广场的演示，其中有更细微的讨论。让你的拥有自己的网站并加入对话！）



原文:

I’ve already had some thoughtful responses to yesterday’s post about trust. I wrapped up my thoughts with a request:

I would love it if someone could explain why they’re avoid native browser features but use third-party code.

Chris obliged:

I can’t speak for the industry, but I have a guess. Third-party code (like the referenced Bootstrap and React) have a history of smoothing over significant cross-browser issues and providing better-than-browser ergonomic APIs. jQuery was created to smooth over cross-browser JavaScript problems. That’s trust.

Very true! jQuery is the canonical example of a library smoothing over the bumpy landscape of browser compatibilities. But jQuery is also the canonical example of a library we no longer need because the browsers have caught up …and those browsers support standards directly influenced by jQuery. That’s a library success story!

Charles Harries takes on my question in his post Libraries over browser features:

I think this perspective of trust has been hammered into developers over the past maybe like 5 years of JavaScript development based almost exclusively on inequality of browser feature support. Things are looking good in 2022; but as recently as 2019, 4 of the 5 top web developer needs had to do with browser compatibility.

Browser compatibility is one of the underlying promises that libraries—especially the big ones that Jeremy references, like React and Bootstrap—make to developers.

So again, it’s browser incompatibilities that made libraries attractive.

Jim Nielsen responds with the same message in his post Trusting Browsers:

We distrust the browser because we’ve been trained to. Years of fighting browser deficiencies where libraries filled the gaps. Browser enemy; library friend.

For example: jQuery did wonders to normalize working across browsers. Write code once, run it in any browser — confidently.

Three for three. My question has been answered: people gravitated towards libraries because browsers had inconsistent implementations.

I’m deliberately using the past tense there. I think Jim is onto something when he says that we’ve been trained not to trust browsers to have parity when it comes to supporting standards. But that has changed.

Charles again:

This approach isn’t a sustainable practice, and I’m trying to do as little of it as I can. Jeremy is right to be suspicious of third-party code. Cross-browser compatibility has gotten a lot better, and campaigns like Interop 2022 are doing a lot to reduce the burden. It’s getting better, but the exasperated I-just-want-it-to-work mindset is tough to uninstall.

I agree. Inertia is a powerful force. No matter how good cross-browser compatibility gets, it’s going to take a long time for developers to shed their suspicion.

Jim is glass-half-full kind of guy:

I’m optimistic that trust in browser-native features and APIs is being restored.

He also points to a very sensible mindset when it comes to third-party libraries and frameworks:

In this sense, third-party code and abstractions can be wonderful polyfills for the web platform. The idea being that the default posture should be: leverage as much of the web platform as possible, then where there are gaps to creating great user experiences, fill them in with exploratory library or framework features (features which, conceivably, could one day become native in browsers).

Yes! A kind of progressive enhancement approach to using third-party code makes a lot of sense. I’ve always maintained that you should treat libraries and frameworks like cattle, not pets. Don’t get too attached. If the library is solving a genuine need, it will be replaced by stable web standards in browsers (again, see jQuery).

I think that third-party libraries and frameworks work best as polyfills. But the whole point of polyfills is that you only use them when the browsers don’t supply features natively (and you also go back and remove the polyfill later when browsers do support the feature). But that’s not how people are using libraries and frameworks today. Developers are reaching for them by default instead of treating them as a last resort.

I like Jim’s proposed design princple:

Where available, default to browser-native features over third party code, abstractions, or idioms.

(P.S. It’s kind of lovely to see this kind of thoughtful blog-to-blog conversation happening. Right at a time when Twitter is about to go down the tubes, this is a demonstration of an actual public square with more nuanced discussion. Make your own website and join the conversation!)

item.title: 相信 | Trust
item.description:
我注意到前端/全栈开发人员中有一种奇怪的心态。 至少我觉得很奇怪。 但也许我是那种心态很奇怪的人，而其他人都知道我不知道的事情。

这与信任和怀疑有关。

我毫不掩饰我对第三方代码和一般依赖项的怀疑这一事实。 您添加到项目中的每个依赖项都是一个潜在的单点故障。 您必须相信编写该代码的陌生人知道他们在做什么。 我仍然有些惊讶的是，开发人员经常添加依赖项——通过 npm 或 yarn 或其他方式——然后引入更多的依赖项，同时假设每个相关人员都具有诚意和能力。

这是对你们人类同胞的一种感人的信仰表达，但我并不热衷于基于信仰的发展理念。

我更信任原生浏览器功能——HTML 元素、CSS 功能和 JavaScript API。 他们并不总是完美的，但他们的发展需要很多思考。 当他们登陆浏览器时，很多聪明人已经开始考虑很多不同的角度。 作为奖励，我不需要安装它们。 更好的是，最终用户不需要安装它们。

然而，我注意到的心态是，许多开发人员对浏览器功能持怀疑态度，但信任第三方库。

当我写和谈论使用service worker时，我经常遇到开发人员对编写 service worker 代码的怀疑。 他们问。 因此，即使一个库可能不提供任何新功能——只是不同的想法——许多开发人员更可能信任第三方库，而不是信任第三方库抽象的底层代码！

开发人员更可能信任 Bootstrap，而不是信任 CSS 网格或自定义属性。 与信任 Web 组件相比，开发人员更可能信任 React。

一方面，我明白了。 这种受欢迎程度足以说明问题。 如果很多人使用一项技术，那一定是一个安全的赌注，对吧？

但是，如果我们谈论流行度，那么今天的每个浏览器都支持网格、自定义属性、服务工作者和 Web 组件等功能。 没有第三方框架甚至可以接近该安装基础。

这些技术已经在稳定的浏览器中发布，这意味着它们已经过审查。 他们经历了严格的测试阶段。 他们有效地得到了每个浏览器制造商的认可。 对我来说，这似乎是比第三方库或框架的流行更大的可信度信号。

因此，我对这种信任第三方代码而不是内置浏览器功能的普遍心态感到有些困惑。

是因为就业市场吗？ 当招聘人员在寻找开发人员时，他们的洗衣清单通常是第三方技术：React、Vue、Bootstrap 等。很难找到列出原生浏览器技术的招聘广告：flexbox、网格、服务工作者、Web 组件。

如果有人能解释为什么他们避免使用本机浏览器功能但使用第三方代码，我会很高兴。

在那之前，我会一直感到困惑。



原文:

I’ve noticed a strange mindset amongst front-end/full-stack developers. At least it seems strange to me. But maybe I’m the one with the strange mindset and everyone else knows something I don’t.

It’s to do with trust and suspicion.

I’ve made no secret of the fact that I’m suspicious of third-party code and dependencies in general. Every dependency you add to a project is one more potential single point of failure. You have to trust that the strangers who wrote that code knew what they were doing. I’m still somewhat flabbergasted that developers regularly add dependencies—via npm or yarn or whatever—that then pull in even more dependencies, all while assuming good faith and competence on the part of every person involved.

It’s a touching expression of faith in your fellow humans, but I’m not keen on the idea of faith-based development.

I’m much more trusting of native browser features—HTML elements, CSS features, and JavaScript APIs. They’re not always perfect, but a lot of thought goes into their development. By the time they land in browsers, a whole lot of smart people have kicked the tyres and considered many different angles. As a bonus, I don’t need to install them. Even better, end users don’t need to install them.

And yet, the mindset I’ve noticed is that many developers are suspicious of browser features but trusting of third-party libraries.

When I write and talk about using service workers, I often come across scepticism from developers about writing the service worker code. “Is there a library I can use?” they ask. “Well, yes” I reply, “but then you’ve got to understand the library, and the time it takes you to do that could be spent understanding the native code.” So even though a library might not offer any new functionality—just a different idion—many developers are more likely to trust the third-party library than they are to trust the underlying code that the third-party library is abstracting!

Developers are more likely to trust, say, Bootstrap than they are to trust CSS grid or custom properties. Developers are more likely to trust React than they are to trust web components.

On the one hand, I get it. Bootstrap and React are very popular. That popularity speaks volumes. If lots of people use a technology, it must be a safe bet, right?

But if we’re talking about popularity, every single browser today ships with support for features like grid, custom properties, service workers and web components. No third-party framework can even come close to that install base.

And the fact that these technologies have shipped in stable browsers means they’re vetted. They’ve been through a rigourous testing phase. They’ve effectively got a seal of approval from each individual browser maker. To me, that seems like a much bigger signal of trustworthiness than the popularity of a third-party library or framework.

So I’m kind of confused by this prevalent mindset of trusting third-party code more than built-in browser features.

Is it because of the job market? When recruiters are looking for developers, their laundry list is usually third-party technologies: React, Vue, Bootstrap, etc. It’s rare to find a job ad that lists native browser technologies: flexbox, grid, service workers, web components.

I would love it if someone could explain why they’re avoid native browser features but use third-party code.

Until then, I shall remain perplexed.
item.author: adactio

item.title: 声明式设计 | Declarative design
item.description:
我觉得在过去的几年里，有许多网页设计方法有着相似的心态。 安迪和海登的每一个布局； 在某种程度上，它们的优势在于 CSS 的技术进步：flexbox、grid、calc 等。 但更重要的是，他们共享一种方法。 他们都专注于创造正确的输入，而不是试图控制每一个可能的输出。 将这些输出的最终计算留给浏览器——这是计算机擅长的。

正如安迪所说：

成为浏览器的导师，而不是它的微观管理者。

吉姆尼尔森在反思乌托邦的方法时写道：

我们说 CSS 是“声明性的”，但我越是写断点来适应设计在视口范围内改变的所有不同方式，我就越觉得我在编写命令式代码。 一组声明性规则在多大程度上开始看起来像命令式指令？

相比之下，乌托邦的原则之一是声明性的，“描述要做什么，而不是命令如何去做”。 这种方法声明了一组规则，以便您可以选择任何视口宽度，并使用公式推导出该大小下的类型大小和间距。

声明性的！ 也许这就是我一直在寻找的词来描述乌托邦、每个布局和内在网页设计之间的共性。

那么，如果声明式设计是一件事，那是否也意味着命令式设计也是一件事呢？ 命令式设计的工具和技术会是什么样子？

我认为Tailwind可能是命令式设计工具的一个很好的例子。 这只是关于特定的输出。 系统性思维被积极劝阻； 相反，您确切地说出您希望屏幕上的最终像素是什么。

我并不是说像 Utopia 这样的声明式工具是正确的，而像 Tailwind 这样的命令式工具是错误的。 一如既往，这取决于。 在这种情况下，这取决于您的心态。

如果你同意这个说法，你可能应该使用命令式设计工具：

但是如果你同意这个说法，你可能应该使用声明式设计工具：

如果您同意第一个陈述，但您尝试使用像 Utopia 或 Every Layout 这样的声明性工具，您可能会遇到麻烦。 你可能会讨厌它。 您可以声明该工具是“坏的”。

同样，如果您同意第二个陈述，但随后尝试使用 Tailwind 之类的命令式工具，您可能会遇到麻烦。 你可能会讨厌它。 您可以声明该工具是“坏的”。

这完全取决于该工具背后的理念是否与您自己的理念相匹配。 如果这些理念相匹配，那么使用该工具将是富有成效的，并且该工具将充当放大器——大脑的自行车。 但是，如果该工具的理念与您自己的理念不符，那么您将在每一步都与该工具作斗争——它会让您慢下来。

当您评估技术时，了解声明式工具和命令式工具之间存在这种范围可以为您提供帮助。 你可以评估一个网页设计工具是在 CSS 被破坏的前提下还是在 CSS 很棒的前提下被推销的。

我想知道你进入网页设计和开发的道路是否也会影响你认同的光谱的哪一端。 比如，如果你的背景是 HTML 和 CSS 之类的声明性语言，也许内在的网页设计真的能引起共鸣。 但是，如果您的背景是 JavaScript 等命令式语言，那么 Tailwind 对您来说可能更有意义。

同样，这里没有对错。 这是关于将正确的工具与正确的心态相匹配。

就个人而言，声明式设计方法非常适合我。 感觉就像是约翰的网页设计之道的传统或 Ethan 的响应式 Web 设计— 使用 Web纹理的方式。



原文:

I feel like in the past few years there’s been a number of web design approaches that share a similar mindset. Intrinsic web design by Jen; Every Layout by Andy and Heydon; Utopia by Trys and James.

To some extent, their strengths lie in technological advances in CSS: flexbox, grid, calc, and so on. But more importantly, they share an approach. They all focus on creating the right inputs rather than trying to control every possible output. Leave the final calculations for those outputs to the browser—that’s what computers are good at.

As Andy puts it:

Be the browser’s mentor, not its micromanager.

Reflecting on Utopia’s approach, Jim Nielsen wrote:

We say CSS is “declarative”, but the more and more I write breakpoints to accommodate all the different ways a design can change across the viewport spectrum, the more I feel like I’m writing imperative code. At what quantity does a set of declarative rules begin to look like imperative instructions?

In contrast, one of the principles of Utopia is to be declarative and “describe what is to be done rather than command how to do it”. This approach declares a set of rules such that you could pick any viewport width and, using a formula, derive what the type size and spacing would be at that size.

Declarative! Maybe that’s the word I’ve been looking for to describe the commonalities between Utopia, Every Layout, and intrinsic web design.

So if declarative design is a thing, does that also mean imperative design is also a thing? And what might the tools and technologies for imperative design look like?

I think that Tailwind might be a good example of an imperative design tool. It’s only about the specific outputs. Systematic thinking is actively discouraged; instead you say exactly what you want the final pixels on the screen to be.

I’m not saying that declarative tools—like Utopia—are right and that imperative tools—like Tailwind—are wrong. As always, it depends. In this case, it depends on the mindset you have.

If you agree with this statement, you should probably use an imperative design tool:

CSS is broken and I want my tools to work around the way CSS has been designed.

But if you agree with this statement, you should probably use a declarative design tool:

CSS is awesome and I want my tools to amplify the way that CSS had been designed.

If you agree with the first statement but you then try using a declarative tool like Utopia or Every Layout, you will probably have a bad time. You’ll probably hate it. You may declare the tool to be “bad”.

Likewise if you agree with the second statement but you then try using an imperative tool like Tailwind, you will probably have a bad time. You’ll probably hate it. You may declare the tool to be “bad”.

It all depends on whether the philosophy behind the tool matches your own philosophy. If those philosophies match up, then using the tool will be productive and that tool will act as an amplifier—a bicycle for the mind. But if the philosophy of the tool doesn’t match your own philosophy, then you will be fighting the tool at every step—it will slow you down.

Knowing that this spectrum exists between declarative tools and imperative tools can help you when you’re evaluating technology. You can assess whether a web design tool is being marketed on the premise that CSS is broken or on the premise that CSS is awesome.

I wonder whether your path into web design and development might also factor into which end of the spectrum you’d identify with. Like, if your background is in declarative languages like HTML and CSS, maybe intrisic web design really resonates. But if your background is in imperative languages like JavaScript, perhaps Tailwind makes more sense to you.

Again, there’s no right or wrong here. This is about matching the right tool to the right mindset.

Personally, the declarative design approach fits me like a glove. It feels like it’s in the tradition of John’s A Dao Of Web Design or Ethan’s Responsive Web Design—ways of working with the grain of the web.

item.title: 零售商如何利用云来保持竞争力 | How retailers can leverage the cloud to stay competitive
item.description:
过去几年对零售业来说是一个巨大的挑战。 由于 Covid 19 大流行的封锁，仅在美国就有创纪录的 12,200 家实体店关闭。 同样，英国大街上的 8,700 家连锁店也关门了。 在这场大屠杀中幸存下来的公司是那些在此过程中或立即采用云等在线技术的公司。

但是仅仅迁移到云端并不能解决您的问题。 您仍将面临诸如高客户期望、技术快速进步和全渠道商务等挑战。 这些变化会给您带来成本压力和不确定性。 面对变化，传统方法可能行不通，这意味着现在是明确定义新目标、改变运营模式和重新思考零售的时候了。 你们中那些现在采取正确行动的人可能会在未来几十年享有持续的优势。

在本博客中，我们将了解零售商如何利用云来保持竞争力。



原文:

Image description Cloud Service

The last couple of years has been a huge challenge for the retail industry. Due to the Covid 19 pandemic lockdowns, a record 12,200 brick-and-mortar stores closed down in the US alone. Similarly, 8,700 chain stores have closed from British high streets. The companies that survived this carnage were the ones who were in the process or immediately embraced online technologies such as the cloud.

But just moving to the cloud does not solve your problems. You will still face challenges such as high customer expectations, rapid advancements in technology, and omnichannel commerce. These changes can create cost pressures and uncertainty for you. Traditional approaches might not work in the face of change, which means now is the time to clearly define new targets, make changes to operating models, and rethink retail. Those of you who make the right moves now may enjoy a sustained advantage for decades to come.

In this blog we are going to see how retailers can leverage the cloud to stay competitive.
